752	\begin{definition}
753	
754	[Chart quotient \(u^I\)]
755	  \label{def:gr_chart_quotient}
756	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap}
757	  \uses{def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd}
758	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
759	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
760	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L898-L910).
761	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
762	  % "We next define a rank $d$ locally free sheaf $\U$ on $\grass(r,d)$
763	  %  together with a surjective homomorphism $\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$.
764	  %  On each $U^I$ we define a surjective homomorphism
765	  %  $u^I :\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I} \to  \oplus^d\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I}$
766	  %  by the matrix $X^I$."
767	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
768	  Let \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\). On the affine chart
769	  \(U^I = \Spec R^I\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) define the
770	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}\)-linear homomorphism of trivial bundles
771	  \[
772	    u^I \;:\; \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}
773	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}
774	  \]
775	  given by left multiplication by the universal \(d \times r\) matrix \(X^I\)
776	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}): on global sections it is the \(R^I\)-linear
777	  map \((R^I)^{r} \to (R^I)^{d}\), \(v \mapsto X^I v\). Since the \(I\)-th minor
778	  \(X^I_I\) of \(X^I\) is the identity matrix \(1_{d \times d}\), the composite of
779	  \(u^I\) with the inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates
780	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \hookrightarrow \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) is the
781	  identity; hence \(u^I\) is a split surjection of locally free sheaves, with
782	  target the free rank-\(d\) sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\).
783	
784	  \emph{Realisation.} The free sheaves \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) and
785	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\) are the finite biproducts (coproducts) of copies of
786	  the unit module \(\mathbf{1} = \mathcal{O}_{U^I}\), indexed by \(\{1,\dots,r\}\)
787	  and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). Each entry \(X^I_{p,q} \in R^I\) of the universal matrix
788	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}) is a global function on \(U^I = \Spec R^I\),
789	  obtained from \(R^I\) through the canonical identification
790	  \(\Gamma(\Spec R^I, \mathcal{O}) \cong R^I\); the corresponding scalar
791	  endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(X^I_{p,q}) : \mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\)
792	  (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is the \((p,q)\)-component of \(u^I\). Assembling these
793	  components over the index biproducts -- the universal property of the biproduct
794	  packaging a matrix of component morphisms into a single morphism of the free
795	  sheaves -- yields \(u^I\). This entry-by-entry assembly via the finite biproduct
796	  is the substitute for a (non-existent) Mathlib ``matrix \(\mapsto\) morphism of
797	  free sheaves'' primitive.
798	\end{definition}
799	
800	\begin{lemma}[The $I$-columns of the chart quotient are the identity]
801	  \label{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_iFree}
802	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap_ιFree}
803	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd,
804	    lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
805	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
806	  For each \(k \in \{1,\dots,d\}\), the chart quotient \(u^I\)
807	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) carries the \(\iota_I(k)\)-th basis section of the
808	  free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) -- where \(\iota_I(k)\) is the \(k\)-th
809	  element of \(I\) in increasing order -- to the \(k\)-th basis section of
810	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\); that is, the \(I\)-indexed columns of \(u^I\) form the
811	  identity:
812	  \[
813	    e_{\iota_I(k)} \,\circ\, u^I \;=\; e_k.
814	  \]
815	\end{lemma}
816	
817	\begin{proof}
818	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd,
819	    lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
820	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
821	  By the biproduct presentation of \(u^I\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}), the
822	  composite \(e_{\iota_I(k)} \circ u^I\) reads off the \(\iota_I(k)\)-th column of the
823	  component matrix, whose \(p\)-th entry is the scalar endomorphism
824	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)})\). The \(I\)-indexed columns of the
825	  universal matrix \(X^I\), read through the order isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \cong
826	  I\), form the \(d \times d\) identity (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}),
827	  so \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)} = \delta_{p,k}\). Hence the diagonal entry is
828	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the
829	  off-diagonal entries are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\)
830	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}); the resulting column is the \(k\)-th coordinate
831	  inclusion, giving \(e_{\iota_I(k)} \circ u^I = e_k\).
832	\end{proof}
833	
834	\begin{lemma}[The chart quotient is an epimorphism]
835	  \label{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi}
836	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap_epi}
837	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient}
838	  The chart quotient \(u^I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
839	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) is an epimorphism of sheaves of modules.
840	\end{lemma}
841	
842	\begin{proof}
843	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_iFree}
844	  We exhibit a section, so that \(u^I\) is a \emph{split} epimorphism and hence an
845	  epimorphism. Let \(\iota_I : \{1,\dots,d\} \hookrightarrow \{1,\dots,r\}\) be the
846	  inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates, \(k \mapsto \iota_I(k)\), the
847	  \(k\)-th element of \(I\) in increasing order. It induces a morphism of free
848	  sheaves \(s_I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\), the
849	  coordinate inclusion sending the \(k\)-th basis section to the
850	  \(\iota_I(k)\)-th. We claim
851	  \[
852	    u^I \circ s_I \;=\; \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}}.
853	  \]
854	  Both sides are morphisms out of the free rank-\(d\) sheaf, which is the coproduct
855	  of \(d\) copies of the unit module; by the universal property of that coproduct
856	  it suffices to check the equality on each coordinate, i.e.\ to compare component
857	  matrices. The \((p,k)\)-component of \(u^I \circ s_I\) is the entry
858	  \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)}\) of the universal matrix at the \(I\)-indexed column, which
859	  is precisely the \(I\)-th minor \(X^I_I\) of \(X^I\). Since that minor is the
860	  \(d \times d\) identity (the columns of \(X^I\) indexed by \(I\), read through the
861	  order isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \cong I\), form the identity block --
862	  \cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}), one has \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)} = \delta_{p,k}\),
863	  so \(u^I \circ s_I\) has the identity component matrix and equals
864	  \(\mathrm{id}\). Hence \(s_I\) splits \(u^I\) and \(u^I\) is a split
865	  epimorphism, in particular an epimorphism.
866	\end{proof}
867	
868	\section{The \(\mathrm{GL}_d\) bundle transition cocycle}
869	\label{sec:grquot_bundle_cocycle}
870	
871	The universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}) is
872	obtained by feeding the gluing primitive \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} the per-chart free
873	rank-\(d\) sheaves \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\), the bundle transitions
874	\(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\), and the two module cocycle hypotheses (C1)/(C2) that primitive
875	requires. The matrix cocycle for the Cramer inverses \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) is already in hand --
876	it is the content of the scheme-level cocycle \cref{lem:gr_cocycle} and the minor identities
877	\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities} -- but the gluing primitive consumes its
878	\emph{module-level} incarnation: an honest isomorphism of sheaves of modules together with
879	its self-identity and triple-overlap multiplicativity. The three blocks of this section
880	package exactly that, decoupling the construction of \(\mathcal{U}\) from the remaining
881	matrix-to-module transport bookkeeping. This is the only net-new infrastructure the
882	universal quotient requires beyond the gluing primitive itself.
883	
884	\begin{lemma}
885	
886	[Self-minor Cramer inverse is the identity]
887	  \label{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_self}
888	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMinorInv_self}
889	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
890	  The Cramer inverse of the self-minor is the identity matrix:
891	  \((X^I_I)^{-1} = 1_{d \times d}\).
892	\end{lemma}
893	\begin{proof}
894	  \uses{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
895	  The self-minor \(X^I_I\) is the identity block
896	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}), and \(1^{-1} = 1\).
897	\end{proof}
898

1	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GrassmannianCells
2	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme
3	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GlueDescent
4	
5	/-!
6	# The tautological quotient and the universal property of `Gr(r,d)`
7	
8	This file adds, on top of the Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)` built in
9	`GrassmannianCells.lean`, the tautological rank-`d` quotient
10	`u : O^r ↠ U` and the universal property making `Gr(d,r)` the fine moduli
11	space of rank-`d` locally free quotients of `O^r`.
12	
13	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianQuot.tex` (Nitsure §1).
14	-/
15	
16	universe u
17	
18	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
19	
20	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
21	
22	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scalar endomorphisms of the structure sheaf
23	
24	To realise a matrix of regular functions as a morphism of free sheaves of modules we
25	need to turn a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` of the structure sheaf into a scalar
26	endomorphism of `O_X` (= `SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`). On the affine chart
27	`U^I = Spec R^I` the matrix entries of the universal matrix `X^I` live in `R^I`, and we
28	inject them into the global sections via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`.
29	
30	These helpers are project-local: Mathlib has no ready-made "matrix ↦ morphism of free
31	sheaves" primitive. -/
32	
33	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
34	
35	/-- The global section of the structure sheaf `O_X` (as a sheaf of modules over itself)
36	determined by a section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` over the whole space, by restriction to every open.
37	Project-local helper for building scalar endomorphisms. -/
38	noncomputable def globalUnitSection (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
39	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).sections :=
40	  PresheafOfModules.sectionsMk
41	    (fun Y => (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
42	    (by
43	      intro Y Z f
44	      change (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f) (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
45	        = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a
46	      rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, ← X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map_comp]
47	      congr 1)
48	
49	/-- The scalar endomorphism of `O_X` given by a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)`:
50	multiplication by `a`. Project-local helper. -/
51	noncomputable def scalarEnd (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
52	    SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
53	  (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.symm (globalUnitSection a)
54	
55	/-- `scalarEnd 1` is the identity endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for
56	identifying the diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
57	lemma scalarEnd_one : scalarEnd (1 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) := by
58	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
59	  ext Y
60	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (1 : Γ(X, ⊤))
61	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val (𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))).app Y
62	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
63	  rw [SheafOfModules.id_val, PresheafOfModules.id_app]
64	  exact map_one _
65	
66	/-- `scalarEnd 0` is the zero endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for identifying
67	the off-diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
68	lemma scalarEnd_zero : scalarEnd (0 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 0 := by
69	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
70	  ext Y
71	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (0 : Γ(X, ⊤))
72	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val
73	          (0 : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).app Y
74	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
75	  refine (map_zero _).trans ?_
76	  rfl
77	
78	/-- The value of the scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` on a section `x` over `Y` is
79	multiplication by the restriction of `a`: `(scalarEnd a)(x) = x · a|_Y`. Project-local
80	helper, the computational heart of the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities below. -/
81	lemma scalarEnd_val_app (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ)
82	    (x : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y) :
83	    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y x = x * X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
84	  rfl
85	
86	/-- `scalarEnd c` corresponds to the global section `c` under `unitHomEquiv`. -/
87	lemma unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd (c : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
88	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd c) = globalUnitSection c := by
89	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
90	
91	/-- The scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` sends the unit section `1` to `a|_Y`. -/
92	lemma scalarEnd_val_app_one (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ) :
93	    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
94	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
95	  exact one_smul _ _
96	
97	/-- Composition of scalar endomorphisms is multiplication: `scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b =
98	scalarEnd (a * b)`. Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` multiplicativity. -/
99	lemma scalarEnd_comp (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
100	    scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b = scalarEnd (a * b) := by
101	  apply (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.injective
102	  change SheafOfModules.sectionsMap (scalarEnd b)
103	        ((SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd a))
104	      = (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd (a * b))
105	  rw [unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd, unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd]
106	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
107	  change (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
108	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a * b)
109	  exact (RingHom.map_mul (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b).symm
110	
111	/-- `scalarEnd` is additive: `scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b`.
112	Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` matrix-multiplication identity. -/
113	lemma scalarEnd_add (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
114	    scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b := by
115	  conv_lhs => rw [scalarEnd]
116	  rw [Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
117	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
118	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a + b)
119	      = (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
120	        + (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
121	  rw [scalarEnd_val_app_one, scalarEnd_val_app_one]
122	  exact RingHom.map_add (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b
123	
124	/-- `scalarEnd` of a finite sum is the sum of the `scalarEnd`s. Project-local. -/
125	lemma scalarEnd_sum {ι : Type*} (s : Finset ι) (f : ι → Γ(X, ⊤)) :
126	    scalarEnd (∑ i ∈ s, f i) = ∑ i ∈ s, scalarEnd (f i) := by
127	  classical
128	  induction s using Finset.induction with
129	  | empty => simp [scalarEnd_zero]
130	  | insert a s ha ih => rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, scalarEnd_add, ih]
131	
132	/-! ## Matrix automorphisms of the free sheaf
133	
134	To realise the `GL_d` bundle transitions we promote an (invertible) `d × d` matrix of
135	global sections to an automorphism of the free rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`, exactly as
136	`chartQuotientMap` realises the universal matrix. The two key algebraic facts — that
137	`matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition and the identity matrix into the
138	identity — follow from the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities above. -/
139	
140	/-- `SheafOfModules` over `O_S` has finite biproducts (it has finite products). -/
141	instance hasFiniteBiproducts_modules (S : Scheme.{0}) :
142	    HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
143	  HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
144	
145	/-- A `d × d` matrix of global sections of `O_S` realised as an endomorphism of the free
146	rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`: the `(p,q)`-entry acts as `scalarEnd`, assembled over the rank-`d`
147	biproduct (mirrors `chartQuotientMap`). Project-local. -/
148	noncomputable def matrixEnd {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
149	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶
150	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
151	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
152	    biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i)) ≫
153	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom
154	
155	/-- Composition of two `biproduct.matrix` morphisms is the matrix of pointwise sums of
156	composites — the categorical matrix product. Project-local helper for `matrixEnd_comp`. -/
157	private lemma biproduct_matrix_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
158	    (mM mN : Fin d → Fin d →
159	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)) :
160	    biproduct.matrix (f := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)
161	        (g := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) mM ≫ biproduct.matrix mN
162	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i q => ∑ p, mM i p ≫ mN p q) := by
163	  refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun q => ?_))
164	  simp only [Category.assoc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.matrix_π, biproduct.lift_desc,
165	    biproduct.ι_matrix, biproduct.lift_π]
166	
167	/-- `matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition (with the order reversed by
168	the contravariance of the column/component indexing): `matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N =
169	matrixEnd (N * M)`. Project-local. -/
170	lemma matrixEnd_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
171	    matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N = matrixEnd (N * M) := by
172	  rw [matrixEnd, matrixEnd, matrixEnd]
173	  have hcomp : biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))
174	        ≫ biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (N p i))
175	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd ((N * M) p i)) := by
176	    rw [biproduct_matrix_comp]
177	    congr 1
178	    funext i q
179	    simp_rw [scalarEnd_comp]
180	    rw [← scalarEnd_sum, Matrix.mul_apply]
181	    exact congrArg scalarEnd (Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun p _ => mul_comm _ _))
182	  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
183	  rw [← Category.assoc (biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))), hcomp]
184	
185	/-- `matrixEnd` of the identity matrix is the identity. Project-local. -/
186	lemma matrixEnd_one {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} :
187	    matrixEnd (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) = 𝟙 _ := by
188	  rw [matrixEnd]
189	  have hmat : biproduct.matrix
190	        (fun i p => scalarEnd ((1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) p i))
191	      = 𝟙 (⨁ fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) := by
192	    refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_))
193	    simp only [Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.lift_π]
194	    rw [Matrix.one_apply]
195	    by_cases h : p = i
196	    · subst h; rw [if_pos rfl, scalarEnd_one, biproduct.ι_π_self]
197	    · rw [if_neg h, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm h)]
198	  rw [hmat, Category.id_comp, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.inv_hom_id]
199	
200	/-- An invertible `d × d` matrix of global sections induces an automorphism of the free
201	rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`. Project-local — the `GL_d` data underlying the bundle transitions. -/
202	noncomputable def matrixToFreeIso {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
203	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
204	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ≅
205	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) where
206	  hom := matrixEnd M
207	  inv := matrixEnd N
208	  hom_inv_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hNM, matrixEnd_one]
209	  inv_hom_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hMN, matrixEnd_one]
210	
211	@[simp] lemma matrixToFreeIso_hom {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
212	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
213	    (matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM).hom = matrixEnd M := rfl
214	
215	/-- **Matrix automorphisms compose multiplicatively** (`lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul`): the
216	forward maps of two matrix automorphisms compose to `matrixEnd` of the matrix product (with
217	the order reversed by the column/component contravariance). This is the linkage that turns
218	the matrix-level Cramer-inverse cocycle into a composition identity of sheaf-of-module
219	isomorphisms. Project-local. -/
220	lemma matrixToFreeIso_mul {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
221	    (A A' B B' : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤))
222	    (hAA' : A * A' = 1) (hA'A : A' * A = 1) (hBB' : B * B' = 1) (hB'B : B' * B = 1) :
223	    (matrixToFreeIso A A' hAA' hA'A).hom ≫ (matrixToFreeIso B B' hBB' hB'B).hom
224	      = matrixEnd (B * A) := by
225	  rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixEnd_comp]
226	
227	@[simp] lemma matrixToFreeIso_inv {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
228	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
229	    (matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM).inv = matrixEnd N := rfl
230	
231	/-! ## The rectangular matrix homomorphism of free sheaves
232	
233	The chart quotient `u^I` is "left multiplication by the `d × r` universal matrix `X^I`";
234	to manipulate it under pullback and against the square `GL_d` transitions we generalise
235	the square `matrixEnd` API to rectangular matrices (`def:gr_matrixEndRect`,
236	`lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp`; the pullback naturality `lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback` comes
237	after the scalar atom below). -/
238	
239	/-- A `d × r` matrix of global sections of `O_S` realised as a morphism of free sheaves
240	`O_S^r ⟶ O_S^d` (`def:gr_matrixEndRect`): the `(p,q)`-entry acts as `scalarEnd`, assembled
241	over the two biproducts exactly as the square `matrixEnd` and the chart quotient
242	`chartQuotientMap` (which is by construction `matrixEndRect` of the injected universal
243	matrix). Project-local. -/
244	noncomputable def matrixEndRect {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
245	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
246	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
247	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
248	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
249	    biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i)) ≫
250	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom
251	
252	/-- Composition of two `biproduct.matrix` morphisms with a rectangular first factor —
253	the categorical matrix product. Project-local helper for `matrixEndRect_comp`. -/
254	private lemma biproduct_matrix_comp_rect {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
255	    (mM : Fin r → Fin d →
256	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf))
257	    (mN : Fin d → Fin d →
258	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)) :
259	    biproduct.matrix (f := fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)
260	        (g := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) mM ≫ biproduct.matrix mN
261	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i q => ∑ p, mM i p ≫ mN p q) := by
262	  refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun q => ?_))
263	  simp only [Category.assoc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.matrix_π, biproduct.lift_desc,
264	    biproduct.ι_matrix, biproduct.lift_π]
265	
266	/-- **Square-after-rectangular composition law** (`lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp`):
267	`matrixEndRect M ≫ matrixEnd N = matrixEndRect (N * M)` for `M : d × r`, `N : d × d` —
268	the matrix product, with the order reversed by the contravariance of the column/component
269	indexing exactly as in `matrixEnd_comp`. Project-local. -/
270	@[reassoc]
271	lemma matrixEndRect_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
272	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)) (N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
273	    matrixEndRect M ≫ matrixEnd N = matrixEndRect (N * M) := by
274	  rw [matrixEndRect, matrixEnd, matrixEndRect]
275	  have hcomp : biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin r) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd (M p i))
276	        ≫ biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin d) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd (N p i))
277	      = biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin r) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd ((N * M) p i)) := by
278	    rw [biproduct_matrix_comp_rect]
279	    congr 1
280	    funext i q
281	    simp_rw [scalarEnd_comp]
282	    rw [← scalarEnd_sum, Matrix.mul_apply]
283	    exact congrArg scalarEnd (Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun p _ => mul_comm _ _))
284	  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
285	  rw [← Category.assoc (biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin r) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd (M p i))),
286	    hcomp]
287	
288	/-! ## The tautological quotient on the charts -/
289	
290	/-- The **chart quotient** `u^I : O_{U^I}^r → O_{U^I}^d` (`def:gr_chart_quotient`):
291	left multiplication by the universal matrix `X^I` (`universalMatrix`). It is realised as
292	the morphism of free sheaves of modules whose matrix of components, in the standard bases
293	`(e_{i'})_{i' : Fin r}` and `(e_p)_{p : Fin d}`, is the universal matrix `X^I` injected
294	into the structure sheaf via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. Since the `I`-minor of `X^I` is the
295	identity, `u^I` is a split surjection onto the free rank-`d` sheaf.
296	
297	Project-local: Mathlib has no "matrix ↦ morphism of free sheaves" primitive. -/
298	noncomputable def chartQuotientMap (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
299	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
300	      SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=

916	  \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in \mathrm{GL}_d(R^I_J)\) (\cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}),
917	  the Cramer inverse of the localised \(J\)-minor, which is invertible because
918	  \(\det(X^I_J)\) is a unit of \(R^I_J\) (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}).
919	
920	  \emph{Realisation.} The transition is built exactly as the chart quotient
921	  \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}: each entry \((X^I_J)^{-1}_{p,q} \in R^I_J\) is a global
922	  function on the overlap, its scalar endomorphism
923	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}_{p,q}\bigr)\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is the
924	  \((p,q)\)-component, and the universal property of the rank-\(d\) biproduct assembles
925	  these into an automorphism of the free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\); the
926	  source and target pullbacks of free sheaves are identified with this
927	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\) through the free-pullback comparisons
928	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} along \(f_{IJ}\) and \(t_{IJ} \circ f_{JI}\), conjugating
929	  the matrix automorphism into the displayed isomorphism. Invertibility of the matrix makes
930	  \(g_{I,J}\) an isomorphism.
931	\end{definition}

933	\begin{lemma}[Self-identity of the bundle transition (C1)]
934	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id}
935	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_self}
936	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
937	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
938	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
939	  The self-transition is the identity: \(g_{I,I} = \mathrm{id}\) on
940	  \(f_{II}^{*}\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\). This is the module-level form of the descent
941	  condition (C1) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} for the bundle data \((g_{I,J})\).
942	\end{lemma}
943	\begin{proof}
944	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
945	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
946	  The \(I\)-th minor of the universal matrix is the identity block, \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\)
947	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}), so its Cramer inverse is again the
948	  identity, \((X^I_I)^{-1} = 1_{d\times d}\) (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}).
949	  Hence in the biproduct presentation of \cref{def:gr_bundleTransition} the diagonal
950	  components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the
951	  off-diagonal components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}), so
952	  the assembled automorphism is the identity of \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_I}^{\,d}\); conjugation by
953	  the free-pullback comparisons preserves the identity, giving \(g_{I,I} = \mathrm{id}\).
954	\end{proof}

957	
958	[Bundle transition data over the glue datum]
959	  \label{def:gr_bundleTransitionData}
960	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransitionData}
961	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, def:scheme_modules_glue}
962	  The family of bundle transitions \((g_{I,J})\) (\cref{def:gr_bundleTransition}), repackaged
963	  over the index set of the Grassmannian glue datum \(\mathrm{theGlueData}(d,r)\) in the exact
964	  shape \(\bigl(\forall I\,J,\; f_{IJ}^{*}\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \cong (t_{IJ} \circ
965	  f_{JI})^{*}\mathcal{O}_{U^J}^{\,d}\bigr)\) that the gluing primitive
966	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} consumes.

969	\begin{lemma}[Cramer-inverse cocycle on the triple overlap]
970	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix}
971	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix}
972	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
973	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col,
974	    lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel, lem:gr_cocycle}
975	  Over the common triple-overlap ring (the localisation carrying the three charts'
976	  data to \(V_{IJK}\)) the Cramer inverses of the localised minors satisfy the
977	  multiplicative cocycle identity
978	  \[
979	    (X^J_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}.
980	  \]
981	  This is the pure matrix-algebra core of (C2), independent of any sheaf data.
982	\end{lemma}
983	\begin{proof}
984	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
985	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col,
986	    lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel, lem:gr_cocycle}
987	  Write all matrices over the common triple-overlap ring. The image matrix of the
988	  transition \(\theta_{I,J}\) is \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\), and its \(K\)-minor is
989	  computed by restricting to the \(K\)-indexed columns; since taking a column
990	  submatrix commutes with left multiplication (\cref{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col}),
991	  \[
992	    X^J_K \;=\; \bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\bigr)_K \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I_K .
993	  \]
994	  Both \(X^I_J\) and \(X^I_K\) have unit determinant
995	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}), so this product of invertibles inverts
996	  by \((AB)^{-1} = B^{-1} A^{-1}\):
997	  \[
998	    (X^J_K)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J).
999	  \]
1000	  Multiplying on the right by \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) and cancelling \(X^I_J (X^I_J)^{-1} = 1\)
1001	  via the two-sided Cramer identity (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}, the
1002	  cancellation packaged in \cref{lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel}) gives
1003	  \((X^J_K)^{-1} (X^I_J)^{-1} = (X^I_K)^{-1}\). The agreement of the three image
1004	  matrices over the triple overlap that licenses these manipulations is the content of
1005	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq}, the matrix form of the scheme-level cocycle
1006	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle}.
1007	\end{proof}
1008	
1009	\begin{lemma}[Scalar endomorphism is natural under pullback]
1010	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}
1011	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_pullback}
1012	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1013	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(a \in \Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S)\) a
1014	  global function, with comorphism on global sections
1015	  \(p^{\sharp} : \Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S) \to \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)\).
1016	  Write \(q : p^{*}\mathbf{1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathbf{1}\) for the unit-pullback comparison
1017	  (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index). Then the pullback of the scalar endomorphism
1018	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) is conjugate, through \(q\), to the scalar endomorphism of the
1019	  base-changed function:
1020	  \[
1021	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\bigr)
1022	      \;=\; q^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl(p^{\sharp} a\bigr) \,\circ\, q
1023	  \]
1024	  as endomorphisms of \(p^{*}\mathbf{1}\); equivalently
1025	  \(q \circ p^{*}(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)) = \mathrm{scalarEnd}(p^{\sharp} a) \circ q\). This is the
1026	  single-entry naturality atom underlying the matrix-level statement
1027	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}.
1028	\end{lemma}
1029	\begin{proof}
1030	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1031	  The scalar endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is multiplication
1032	  by the global section \(a\) on the unit module \(\mathbf 1 = \mathcal{O}_S\). Pulling back
1033	  along \(p\) sends multiplication by \(a\) to multiplication by its image, and the comorphism
1034	  \(p^{\sharp}\) is precisely the action of pullback on global functions, so
1035	  \(p^{*}(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a))\) is multiplication by \(p^{\sharp}a\) on \(p^{*}\mathbf 1\). The
1036	  comparison \(q\) is an isomorphism of unit modules intertwining this multiplication on
1037	  \(p^{*}\mathbf 1\) with multiplication by \(p^{\sharp}a\) on \(\mathbf 1\); conjugating back
1038	  along \(q\) therefore yields the claimed identity. The verification is a single computation on
1039	  the unit module, where multiplication by a function commutes with the natural comparison
1040	  \(q\).
1041	\end{proof}
1042	
1043	\begin{lemma}[Matrix endomorphism is natural under pullback]
1044	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}
1045	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_pullback}
1046	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1047	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
1048	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes, \(d \in \mathbb{N}\), and
1049	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\). Let
1050	  \(p^{\sharp}M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T))\) be the entrywise
1051	  image of \(M\) under the comorphism \(p^{\sharp}\), and let
1052	  \(Q : p^{*}(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) be the free-pullback
1053	  comparison \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at the rank-\(d\) index set. Then the
1054	  pullback of the matrix endomorphism \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is conjugate, through \(Q\), to
1055	  the matrix endomorphism of the base-changed matrix:
1056	  \[
1057	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\bigr)
1058	      \;=\; Q^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEnd}(p^{\sharp}M) \,\circ\, Q .
1059	  \]
1060	\end{lemma}
1061	\begin{proof}
1062	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1063	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
1064	  By \cref{def:gr_matrixEnd}, \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is the biproduct-assembled endomorphism
1065	  of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} = \bigoplus_{k} \mathbf 1\) whose \((k,\ell)\)-component is
1066	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{k,\ell})\). The pullback functor \(p^{*}\) is a left adjoint, hence
1067	  additive and biproduct-preserving; through the coproduct comparison underlying the
1068	  free-pullback isomorphism \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} it carries the biproduct presentation
1069	  of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) to that of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) and the assembled morphism to the
1070	  morphism reassembled from the pulled-back components, the reassociation of the iterated
1071	  pullbacks being \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. On each single \((k,\ell)\)-component the
1072	  pulled-back scalar endomorphism is, by \cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}, the
1073	  comparison-conjugate of \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(p^{\sharp}M_{k,\ell}) =
1074	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}((p^{\sharp}M)_{k,\ell})\). Reassembling over the biproduct, the conjugating
1075	  one-element comparisons combine into the single free comparison \(Q\), giving
1076	  \(p^{*}(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)) = Q^{-1}\circ\mathrm{matrixEnd}(p^{\sharp}M)\circ Q\).
1077	\end{proof}

1079	\begin{lemma}[Affine global-sections comorphism is the inducing ring homomorphism]
1080	  \label{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec}
1081	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec}
1082	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme}
1083	  Let \(\varphi : A \to B\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and
1084	  \(\Spec\varphi : \Spec B \to \Spec A\) the induced morphism of affine schemes. Under the
1085	  natural identification of the global sections of an affine scheme with its coordinate ring
1086	  -- the counit \(\Gamma(\Spec A, \mathcal O) \xrightarrow{\sim} A\) of the
1087	  \(\Gamma \dashv \Spec\) adjunction, and likewise for \(B\) -- the global-sections base-change
1088	  comorphism
1089	  \[
1090	    (\Spec\varphi)^{\sharp} : \Gamma(\Spec A, \mathcal O) \longrightarrow \Gamma(\Spec B, \mathcal O)
1091	  \]
1092	  is identified with \(\varphi\) itself; that is, the square relating
1093	  \((\Spec\varphi)^{\sharp}\) to \(\varphi\) through the two counit isomorphisms commutes.
1094	\end{lemma}
1095	\begin{proof}
1096	  This is the naturality of the counit isomorphism \(\Gamma \circ \Spec \cong \mathrm{id}\) of
1097	  the global-sections / spectrum adjunction: for any ring homomorphism \(\varphi\) the comorphism
1098	  of \(\Spec\varphi\) on global sections is, after transport through the natural counit
1099	  isomorphisms at \(A\) and \(B\), the homomorphism \(\varphi\). No Grassmannian-specific data
1100	  enters; the statement is the affine-scheme naturality square applied to a single ring map.
1101	\end{proof}

1310	[The universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\)]
1311	  \label{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}
1312	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalQuotient}
1313	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universalMinorInv, def:gr_transition,
1314	    lem:gr_cocycle, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:scheme_modules_glue,
1315	    def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso, def:gr_bundleTransition,
1316	    lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_mul,
1317	    lem:gr_glueData_bridges, def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
1318	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1319	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
1320	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L903-L910).
1321	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1322	  % "Compatible with the co-cycle $(\theta_{I,J})$
1323	  %  for gluing the affine pieces $U^I$, we give gluing data
1324	  %  $(g_{I,J})$ for gluing together the trivial bundles $\oplus^d\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I}$
1325	  %  by putting
1326	  %  $$g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in GL_d(U^I_J)$$"
1327	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
1328	  Over each overlap \(U^I_J = \Spec R^I_J\) define the bundle-transition matrix
1329	  \[
1330	    g_{I,J} \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} \;\in\; \mathrm{GL}_d(R^I_J),
1331	  \]
1332	  the Cramer inverse of the localised \(J\)-minor (\cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}),
1333	  which is invertible because \(\det(X^I_J)\) is a unit of \(R^I_J\). The matrices
1334	  \((g_{I,J})\) are compatible with the chart-gluing cocycle \((\theta_{I,J})\)
1335	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{lem:gr_cocycle}): over a triple overlap they
1336	  satisfy \(g_{I,K} = g_{J,K}\, g_{I,J}\) and \(g_{I,I} = 1\), the multiplicative
1337	  cocycle condition for a vector bundle. Gluing the free rank-\(d\) sheaves
1338	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\) along the isomorphisms \(g_{I,J}\) over the cover
1339	  \(\{U^I\}\) of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) therefore
1340	  produces a locally free \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}\)-module
1341	  \(\mathcal{U}\) of rank \(d\). Its determinant line bundle \(\det \mathcal{U}\)
1342	  has transition functions \(\det(g_{I,J}) = 1/P^I_J\), the data underlying the
1343	  Pl\"ucker embedding.
1344	\end{definition}

1346	The tautological quotient \(u\) is assembled from the chart quotients \(u^I\) by the
1347	section-level descent \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom}; its one remaining content is the
1348	\emph{overlap-compatibility} of the family \((u^I)\) with the bundle gluing
1349	\((g_{I,J})\). The next four blocks isolate that compatibility: a rectangular analogue of
1350	the square matrix-endomorphism API (\cref{def:gr_matrixEnd},
1351	\cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}) for the
1352	\(r \to d\) chart quotient (\cref{def:gr_matrixEndRect},
1353	\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}), the
1354	adjunction transposition that turns the descent condition into a pullback-level identity
1355	(\cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose}), and the matrix-core assembly
1356	that closes it (\cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}).
1357	
1358	\subsection{Matrix-calculus toolbox}
1359	
1360	The chart-cover lemma (\cref{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover}) and the overlap compatibility of
1361	\cref{def:grPointOfRankQuotient} both rest on a small calculus of \emph{rectangular} matrix
1362	homomorphisms of free sheaves, generalising the square API
1363	(\cref{def:gr_matrixEnd}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}). The
1364	blocks below collect that toolbox: the bridge to the square case, the identity and
1365	composition laws, injectivity of the matrix presentation, the column-restriction law, the
1366	affine splitting of epimorphisms of free sheaves and the resulting matrix right inverse, the
1367	field-level minor extraction, and the presented-matrix machinery that packages a quotient as
1368	a matrix along a morphism and proves Nitsure's change-of-basis identity. They are
1369	project-bespoke infrastructure.
1370	
1371	\begin{lemma}[Square and rectangular matrix homomorphisms coincide]
1372	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect}
1373	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect}
1374	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1375	  For a square matrix \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), the
1376	  square and rectangular matrix homomorphisms agree:
1377	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M) = \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\).
1378	\end{lemma}
1379	\begin{proof}
1380	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1381	  Both are the morphism assembled from the same biproduct components
1382	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{k,\ell})\) over the index set \(\{1,\dots,d\}\); the two
1383	  constructions are literally the same, so the equality holds by definition.
1384	\end{proof}
1385	
1386	\begin{lemma}[Identity law for \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\)]
1387	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}
1388	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_one}
1389	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, lem:gr_matrixEnd_one, lem:gr_matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect}
1390	  The rectangular homomorphism of the identity matrix is the identity morphism:
1391	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(1) = \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}}\).
1392	\end{lemma}
1393	\begin{proof}
1394	  \uses{lem:gr_matrixEnd_one, lem:gr_matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect}
1395	  By \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect} the left side equals \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(1)\),
1396	  which is the identity by \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}.
1397	\end{proof}

1399	\begin{lemma}[Fully rectangular composition law]
1400	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect}
1401	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_comp_rect}
1402	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_sum,
1403	    lem:gr_scalarEnd_add, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}
1404	  For \(A \in \operatorname{Mat}_{e\times n}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\) and
1405	  \(B \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times e}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), composing two
1406	  rectangular homomorphisms realises the matrix product, the order reversed by the
1407	  contravariant component indexing exactly as in \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}: the
1408	  composite
1409	  \[
1410	    \mathcal{O}_S^{\,n} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(A)} \mathcal{O}_S^{\,e}
1411	      \xrightarrow{\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(B)} \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}
1412	  \]
1413	  equals \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(B \cdot A)\) (the \(d \times n\) product).
1414	\end{lemma}
1415	\begin{proof}
1416	  \uses{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_sum, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
1417	  The same biproduct-extensionality computation as \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}, with the
1418	  middle biproduct indexed by \(\{1,\dots,e\}\). The \((k,\ell)\)-component of the composite
1419	  is \(\sum_m \mathrm{scalarEnd}(B_{k,m})\circ\mathrm{scalarEnd}(A_{m,\ell})\); each composite
1420	  is the scalar endomorphism of the product (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp}) and a finite sum
1421	  of scalar endomorphisms is the scalar endomorphism of the sum (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_sum},
1422	  \cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}), so the component is
1423	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}((B\cdot A)_{k,\ell})\), the \((k,\ell)\)-component of
1424	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(B\cdot A)\).
1425	\end{proof}
1426	
1427	\begin{lemma}[Uniqueness of the presenting matrix]
1428	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}
1429	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_injective}
1430	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, def:gr_scalarEnd}
1431	  The matrix presentation is injective: if
1432	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M) = \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N)\) for
1433	  \(M, N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), then \(M = N\).
1434	\end{lemma}
1435	\begin{proof}
1436	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
1437	  Extract the \((p,j)\)-entry by pre-composing with the free-basis inclusion of the
1438	  \(j\)-th summand and post-composing with the projection onto the \(p\)-th summand: this
1439	  isolates the scalar endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{p,j})\) from
1440	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\). Equal homomorphisms therefore give
1441	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{p,j}) = \mathrm{scalarEnd}(N_{p,j})\); and \(a \mapsto
1442	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) is injective (its inverse reads off the underlying section), whence
1443	  \(M_{p,j} = N_{p,j}\) for all \(p, j\).
1444	\end{proof}
1445	
1446	\begin{lemma}[Column restriction against the index inclusion]
1447	  \label{lem:gr_freeMap_matrixEndRect}
1448	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.freeMap_matrixEndRect}
1449	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1450	  For an index map \(g : \{1,\dots,e\} \to \{1,\dots,r\}\) and
1451	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), pre-composing the
1452	  rectangular homomorphism with the free index inclusion
1453	  \(\mathrm{freeMap}(g) : \mathcal{O}_S^{\,e} \to \mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}\) restricts to the
1454	  \(g\)-columns:
1455	  \[
1456	    \mathrm{freeMap}(g) \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)
1457	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEndRect}\bigl(M_{\bullet,\, g}\bigr),
1458	  \]
1459	  where \(M_{\bullet,\, g}\) is the \(d \times e\) submatrix with \(\ell\)-th column the
1460	  \(g(\ell)\)-th column of \(M\).
1461	\end{lemma}
1462	\begin{proof}
1463	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1464	  Both sides agree on each free generator \(j\): the inclusion sends it to the \(g(j)\)-th
1465	  generator, and reading off the \((p,j)\)-entry on either side gives \(M_{p, g(j)}\). Cofan
1466	  extensionality over the \(e\) generators concludes.
1467	\end{proof}
1468	
1469	\begin{lemma}[Epimorphisms of free sheaves split over an affine]
1470	  \label{lem:gr_exists_section_of_epi_free_spec}
1471	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.exists_section_of_epi_free_spec}
1472	  On an affine base \(\Spec R\), every epimorphism
1473	  \(\psi : \mathcal{O}_{\Spec R}^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{O}_{\Spec R}^{\,d}\) of
1474	  free sheaves splits: there is a section
1475	  \(\Phi : \mathcal{O}_{\Spec R}^{\,d} \to \mathcal{O}_{\Spec R}^{\,r}\) with
1476	  \(\psi \circ \Phi = \mathrm{id}\).
1477	\end{lemma}
1478	\begin{proof}
1479	  Under the fully faithful additive functor \(\mathrm{Mod}_R \to (\Spec R)\text{-Mod}\)
1480	  taking a module to its associated quasi-coherent sheaf, the free sheaves correspond to the
1481	  free modules \(R^{\,r}, R^{\,d}\) and \(\psi\) to a surjection of modules. A free module is
1482	  projective, so this surjection admits a module-level section; transporting that section
1483	  back through full faithfulness produces the required \(\Phi\).
1484	\end{proof}
1485	
1486	\begin{lemma}[Matrix right inverse over \(\Spec R\)]
1487	  \label{lem:gr_exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec}
1488	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec}
1489	  \uses{lem:gr_exists_section_of_epi_free_spec, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect,
1490	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}
1491	  For \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(\Spec R, \mathcal{O}))\) with
1492	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) an epimorphism, the matrix admits a right inverse: there is
1493	  \(G \in \operatorname{Mat}_{r\times d}(\Gamma(\Spec R, \mathcal{O}))\) with \(M \cdot G = 1\).
1494	\end{lemma}
1495	\begin{proof}
1496	  \uses{lem:gr_exists_section_of_epi_free_spec, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect,
1497	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}
1498	  Split the epimorphism by \cref{lem:gr_exists_section_of_epi_free_spec} and read its section
1499	  off as a matrix \(G\), so that \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(G)\) is the section. The composition
1500	  law (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect}) gives \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M\cdot G)\) as the
1501	  composite of section and epimorphism, which is the identity; with
1502	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(1) = \mathrm{id}\) (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}) and injectivity
1503	  of the presentation (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}) this forces \(M \cdot G = 1\).
1504	\end{proof}
1505	
1506	\begin{lemma}[Matrix right inverse over an affine scheme]
1507	  \label{lem:gr_exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect}
1508	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect}
1509	  \uses{lem:gr_exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}
1510	  For an affine scheme \(S\) and
1511	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) with
1512	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) an epimorphism, there is
1513	  \(G \in \operatorname{Mat}_{r\times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) with \(M \cdot G = 1\).
1514	\end{lemma}
1515	\begin{proof}
1516	  \uses{lem:gr_exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}
1517	  Transport along the canonical isomorphism \(S \cong \Spec\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S)\). The
1518	  base-change naturality of \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback})
1519	  identifies the pulled-back homomorphism, through the free-pullback comparisons, with
1520	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) of the transported matrix, reducing the claim to the \(\Spec\)
1521	  case (\cref{lem:gr_exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect_spec}); pulling the resulting
1522	  right inverse back along the (invertible) global-sections map gives \(G\) over \(S\).
1523	\end{proof}

1525	\begin{lemma}[Field-level minor extraction]
1526	  \label{lem:gr_exists_isUnit_submatrix}
1527	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.exists_isUnit_submatrix}
1528	  Let \(K\) be a field, \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(K)\) and
1529	  \(G \in \operatorname{Mat}_{r\times d}(K)\) with \(M \cdot G = 1\). Then some size-\(d\)
1530	  column-index set \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) gives an invertible minor: the \(d \times d\)
1531	  submatrix \(M_I\) of the \(I\)-columns (in their natural order) is a unit.
1532	\end{lemma}
1533	\begin{proof}
1534	  The right inverse makes the column map \(K^{\,r} \to K^{\,d}\) surjective, so the columns of
1535	  \(M\) span \(K^{\,d}\). A spanning family contains a basis; choosing \(d\) columns forming a
1536	  basis, the corresponding \(d \times d\) submatrix has linearly independent columns and is
1537	  therefore invertible.
1538	\end{proof}

1540	\begin{lemma}[Inverse comparison intertwines the index inclusion]
1541	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_inv_freeMap}
1542	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.pullbackFreeIso_inv_freeMap}
1543	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1544	  For a morphism \(p : W \to V\) and an index map \(g : \{1,\dots,n\} \to \{1,\dots,m\}\), the
1545	  inverse free-pullback comparisons \(Q_n, Q_m\) (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) intertwine the
1546	  index inclusions:
1547	  \[
1548	    Q_n^{-1} \,\circ\, p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{freeMap}(g)\bigr)
1549	      \;=\; \mathrm{freeMap}(g) \,\circ\, Q_m^{-1}.
1550	  \]
1551	\end{lemma}
1552	\begin{proof}
1553	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1554	  This is the inverse form of the forward naturality of \(\mathrm{freeMap}\) under pullback:
1555	  cancel the comparison isomorphisms \(Q_n, Q_m\) on both sides and apply the forward
1556	  intertwining identity \(p^{*}(\mathrm{freeMap}(g))\circ Q_m = Q_n \circ \mathrm{freeMap}(g)\).
1557	\end{proof}

1559	\begin{definition}[Presented matrix of a quotient along a morphism]
1560	  \label{def:gr_presentedMatrix}
1561	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.presentedMatrix}
1562	  \uses{def:gr_rankQuotient, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1563	  Let \(x = \langle\mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) be a rank-\(d\) quotient of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)
1564	  (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}), \(j : P \to T\) a morphism, and \(I\) a size-\(d\) subset such
1565	  that the pulled-back chart composite \(j^{*}(s_I \circ q)\) is an isomorphism. The
1566	  \emph{presented matrix} \(\mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, I) \in
1567	  \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(P, \mathcal{O}_P))\) is the matrix that reads off,
1568	  entrywise via the section-extraction \(\mathrm{unitEndSection}\), the conjugated free-sheaf
1569	  homomorphism
1570	  \[
1571	    Q_r^{-1} \,\circ\, j^{*}q \,\circ\, \bigl(j^{*}(s_I \circ q)\bigr)^{-1}
1572	      \,\circ\, Q_d
1573	      \;:\; \mathcal{O}_P^{\,r} \longrightarrow \mathcal{O}_P^{\,d},
1574	  \]
1575	  where \(Q_r, Q_d\) are the free-pullback comparisons (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}). It
1576	  generalises the chart matrix (the case where \(j\) is the inclusion of the chart locus
1577	  \(T_I\)) and is the vehicle of the overlap comparison.
1578	\end{definition}
1579	
1580	\begin{lemma}[The presented matrix presents]
1581	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix}
1582	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix}
1583	  \uses{def:gr_presentedMatrix, def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1584	  With the data of \cref{def:gr_presentedMatrix},
1585	  \[
1586	    \mathrm{matrixEndRect}\bigl(\mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, I)\bigr)
1587	      \;=\; Q_r^{-1} \,\circ\, j^{*}q \,\circ\, \bigl(j^{*}(s_I \circ q)\bigr)^{-1}
1588	      \,\circ\, Q_d.
1589	  \]
1590	\end{lemma}
1591	\begin{proof}
1592	  \uses{def:gr_presentedMatrix}
1593	  Immediate from the definition: \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) of the
1594	  \(\mathrm{unitEndSection}\)-matrix of a free-sheaf homomorphism recovers that homomorphism.
1595	\end{proof}

1597	\begin{lemma}[Minor of the presented matrix presents the \(J\)-chart composite]
1598	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor}
1599	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor}
1600	  \uses{lem:gr_freeMap_matrixEndRect, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_inv_freeMap,
1601	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix}
1602	  For a second size-\(d\) subset \(J\), the \(J\)-minor of the \(I\)-presented matrix presents
1603	  the pulled-back \(J\)-chart composite against the inverted \(I\)-chart composite:
1604	  \[
1605	    \mathrm{matrixEndRect}\bigl(\mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, I)_{\bullet,\, J}\bigr)
1606	      \;=\; Q_d^{-1} \,\circ\, j^{*}(s_J \circ q) \,\circ\,
1607	        \bigl(j^{*}(s_I \circ q)\bigr)^{-1} \,\circ\, Q_d.
1608	  \]
1609	\end{lemma}
1610	\begin{proof}
1611	  \uses{lem:gr_freeMap_matrixEndRect, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_inv_freeMap,
1612	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix}
1613	  Column restriction (\cref{lem:gr_freeMap_matrixEndRect}) turns the \(J\)-minor into
1614	  pre-composition with the index inclusion \(s_J\); the inverse-comparison intertwiner
1615	  (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_inv_freeMap}) and the presentation identity
1616	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix}) move that inclusion across the pullback, where
1617	  \(s_J \circ q\) is exactly the \(J\)-chart composite.
1618	\end{proof}

1620	\begin{lemma}[Change of basis between chart presentations]
1621	  \label{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_changeOfBasis}
1622	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.presentedMatrix_changeOfBasis}
1623	  \uses{def:gr_presentedMatrix, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix,
1624	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect,
1625	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}
1626	  Along a morphism \(j\) on which both the \(I\)- and \(J\)-chart composites pull back to
1627	  isomorphisms, the \(I\)-presented matrix is the product of its own \(J\)-minor with the
1628	  \(J\)-presented matrix -- Nitsure's overlap identity \(M^I = M^I_J \cdot M^J\):
1629	  \[
1630	    \mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, I)
1631	      \;=\; \mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, I)_{\bullet,\, J}
1632	        \,\cdot\, \mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, J).
1633	  \]
1634	\end{lemma}
1635	\begin{proof}
1636	  \uses{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor,
1637	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}
1638	  Apply \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) and use injectivity (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}).
1639	  By the composition law (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect}) the right side becomes the
1640	  composite of the two presented homomorphisms; substituting
1641	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix} (for both factors) and
1642	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor} (for the minor), the pulled-back
1643	  \(J\)-chart composite \(j^{*}(s_J \circ q)\) cancels with its inverse, leaving
1644	  \(Q_r^{-1} \circ j^{*}q \circ (j^{*}(s_I \circ q))^{-1} \circ Q_d =
1645	  \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(\mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, I))\).
1646	\end{proof}

1648	\begin{lemma}[Invertible presentation forces a unit matrix]
1649	  \label{lem:gr_isUnit_of_isIso_matrixEndRect}
1650	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isUnit_of_isIso_matrixEndRect}
1651	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect,
1652	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}
1653	  Converse of \cref{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso}: if \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N)\) is an
1654	  isomorphism for a square \(N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\),
1655	  then \(N\) is a unit (an invertible matrix).
1656	\end{lemma}
1657	\begin{proof}
1658	  \uses{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective,
1659	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}
1660	  Read off the inverse morphism by its \(\mathrm{unitEndSection}\) matrix \(N'\), so that
1661	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N') = \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N)^{-1}\). The composition law
1662	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp_rect}), the identity law
1663	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}) and injectivity (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective})
1664	  turn the two-sided inverse relation of morphisms into \(N \cdot N' = 1 = N' \cdot N\),
1665	  exhibiting \(N\) as a unit.
1666	\end{proof}

1668	\begin{definition}
1669	
1670	[Rectangular matrix homomorphism of free sheaves]
1671	  \label{def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1672	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect}
1673	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
1674	  Let \(S\) be a scheme, \(d, r \in \mathbb{N}\), and
1675	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times r}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) a rectangular matrix
1676	  of global functions. The \emph{rectangular matrix homomorphism}
1677	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) is the morphism of free sheaves
1678	  \[
1679	    \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M) \;:\; \mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}
1680	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}
1681	  \]
1682	  whose \((k,\ell)\)-component -- \(k \in \{1,\dots,d\}\) an output index,
1683	  \(\ell \in \{1,\dots,r\}\) an input index -- under the presentation of
1684	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}\) and \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) as finite biproducts of copies of the
1685	  unit module \(\mathbf 1\), is the scalar endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{k,\ell})\)
1686	  (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}). It is assembled from these components by the universal property
1687	  of the two biproducts, exactly as the square \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\)
1688	  (\cref{def:gr_matrixEnd}) and the chart quotient (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}). When
1689	  \(r = d\) it specialises to \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\); and the chart quotient \(u^I\)
1690	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) is by construction \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(X^I)\) of the
1691	  universal \(d \times r\) matrix \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}).
1692	\end{definition}
1693	
1694	\begin{lemma}
1695	
1696	[Rectangular matrix homomorphism is natural under pullback]
1697	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}
1698	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_pullback}
1699	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback,
1700	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}
1701	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and
1702	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), with entrywise
1703	  base-changed matrix \(p^{\sharp}M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(T,\mathcal{O}_T))\).
1704	  Write \(Q_r : p^{*}\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\) and
1705	  \(Q_d : p^{*}\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) for the
1706	  free-pullback comparisons (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) at the index sets
1707	  \(\{1,\dots,r\}\) and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). Then the pullback of
1708	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) is conjugate, through \(Q_r\) and \(Q_d\), to the
1709	  rectangular homomorphism of the base-changed matrix:
1710	  \[
1711	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\bigr)
1712	      \;=\; Q_d^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(p^{\sharp}M) \,\circ\, Q_r .
1713	  \]
1714	\end{lemma}
1715	\begin{proof}
1716	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1717	    def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}
1718	  Identical to the square case \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}, with the source and target
1719	  biproducts now indexed by \(\{1,\dots,r\}\) and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\) respectively. The
1720	  pullback functor \(p^{*}\), a left adjoint, is additive and biproduct-preserving; through
1721	  the coproduct comparisons underlying the free-pullback isomorphisms
1722	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} it carries the biproduct presentation of

1733	\begin{lemma}
1734	
1735	[Square-after-rectangular composition law]
1736	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}
1737	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_comp}
1738	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add,
1739	    lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
1740	  For \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\) and
1741	  \(N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), post-composing the
1742	  rectangular homomorphism with a square one realises the matrix product, with the order
1743	  reversed by the contravariance of the component indexing exactly as in
1744	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}:
1745	  \[
1746	    \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M) \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)
1747	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N \cdot M),
1748	  \]
1749	  the composite \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)}
1750	  \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)} \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) (here
1751	  \(N \cdot M\) is the \(d \times r\) product).
1752	\end{lemma}
1753	\begin{proof}

1757	  change being that the leftmost (input) biproduct is indexed by \(\{1,\dots,r\}\) rather
1758	  than \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). The composite of two biproduct-assembled morphisms is again
1759	  biproduct-assembled, with \((k,\ell)\)-component the sum over the middle index \(m\) of the
1760	  composites of components, \(\sum_m \mathrm{scalarEnd}(N_{k,m}) \circ
1761	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{m,\ell})\). Each composite of scalar endomorphisms is the scalar
1762	  endomorphism of the product (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp}) and a finite sum of scalar
1763	  endomorphisms is the scalar endomorphism of the sum (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}); hence
1764	  that component is \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(\sum_m N_{k,m} M_{m,\ell}) =
1765	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}((N\cdot M)_{k,\ell})\), the \((k,\ell)\)-component of
1766	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N\cdot M)\).

1875	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) are compatible with the bundle gluing

1882	  \(\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}). At the
1883	  module level this overlap compatibility -- the equalising condition consumed by the
1884	  section-level descent \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom} -- is exactly
1885	  \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}, whose proof routes the matrix identity above

1890	  \(\mathcal{U}\), agree on overlaps and glue to a single surjective

1904	\section{The functor of points}
1905	\label{sec:grquot_functor}
1906	
1907	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme \(T\) to the set of \emph{equivalence classes} of
1908	rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). The following block packages a single such
1909	quotient, the equivalence relation on them, and the pullback action through which the
1910	functor acts on morphisms; the two coherence blocks after it isolate the comparison
1911	identities that make that action functorial.
1912	
1913	\begin{definition}[Rank-\(d\) quotients and their pullback action]
1914	  \label{def:gr_rankQuotient}
1915	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient,
1916	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.Rel,
1917	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_refl,
1918	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_symm,
1919	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_trans,
1920	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqSetoid,
1921	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback,
1922	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback_rel}
1923	  \uses{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1924	        def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
1925	  A \emph{rank-\(d\) quotient of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} on a scheme \(T\) is a triple
1926	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-modules
1927	  \(\mathcal{F}\) that is locally free of rank \(d\) (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank})
1928	  together with an epimorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) out
1929	  of the trivial rank-\(r\) bundle. Two such quotients \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\)
1930	  and \(y = \langle \mathcal{F}', q'\rangle\) are \emph{equivalent}, written \(x \sim y\),
1931	  when there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with
1932	  \(q \circ f = q'\) (equivalently \(\ker q = \ker q'\)). This relation is reflexive
1933	  (\(f = \mathrm{id}\)), symmetric (\(f \mapsto f^{-1}\)), and transitive (composition of the
1934	  witnessing isomorphisms), hence an equivalence relation -- the setoid whose quotient is the
1935	  functor's value at \(T\).
1936	
1937	  For a morphism \(\psi : T' \to T\) the \emph{pullback action} sends
1938	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) to
1939	  \[
1940	    \psi^{*}x \;=\;
1941	      \bigl\langle\, \psi^{*}\mathcal{F},\;
1942	        \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
1943	        \psi^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\bigr) \xrightarrow{\ \psi^{*}q\ }
1944	        \psi^{*}\mathcal{F} \,\bigr\rangle,
1945	  \]
1946	  re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via the free-pullback isomorphism
1947	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}. The result is again a rank-\(d\) quotient: the composite is
1948	  an epimorphism because pullback, a left adjoint, preserves epimorphisms and the
1949	  re-presentation is an isomorphism, and \(\psi^{*}\mathcal{F}\) is locally free of rank \(d\)
1950	  by \cref{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}. The pullback action respects the equivalence
1951	  relation -- given \(f\) witnessing \(x \sim y\), the pulled-back isomorphism \(\psi^{*}f\)
1952	  witnesses \(\psi^{*}x \sim \psi^{*}y\), by functoriality of pullback -- so it descends to a
1953	  map of equivalence classes.
1954	\end{definition}
1955	
1956	The two blocks below record the comparison identities, between the free-pullback isomorphism
1957	\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} and Mathlib's pseudofunctor structure on pullback, that make
1958	the descended pullback action of \cref{def:gr_rankQuotient} functorial.
1959	
1960	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1961	[Identity coherence of the free-pullback comparison]
1962	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1963	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1964	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1965	  For the identity morphism \(\mathrm{id}_T\), the canonical comparison morphism of unit
1966	  modules \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*}\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\) -- the map underlying the
1967	  free-pullback isomorphism \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index -- coincides
1968	  with the component at \(\mathbf{1}\) of the pullback-identity isomorphism
1969	  \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*} \cong \mathrm{id}\).
1970	\end{lemma}
1971	
1972	\begin{proof}
1973	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1974	  \leanok
1975	  The comparison morphism is the mate of \(\mathrm{id}_{\mathbf 1}\) under the
1976	  pullback--pushforward adjunction: the image of the identity of \(\mathbf 1\) through the
1977	  adjunction's hom-equivalence on the unit. For the identity scheme morphism the pushforward
1978	  is the identity functor, so the adjunction unit is itself an identity; Mathlib's
1979	  pullback-identity isomorphism is exactly the conjugate (left-adjoint transpose) of the
1980	  pushforward-identity isomorphism. Tracing the identity through the hom-equivalence and the
1981	  conjugate-transpose identity for the pullback-identity isomorphism shows the comparison
1982	  equals the \(\mathbf 1\)-component of that isomorphism.
1983	\end{proof}
