2074	upgrade to the corresponding free-sheaf coherences -- the free-pullback isomorphism at the
2075	identity equals the component of the pullback-identity isomorphism, and likewise for the
2076	composite -- by coproduct extensionality over the decomposition
2077	\(\mathcal{O}^{\,\oplus I} = \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\). These coherences discharge both
2078	functoriality laws (identity and composition) of the Grassmannian
2079	functor \cref{def:grassmannian_functor}.
2080	
2081	\begin{definition}
2082	
2083	[The Grassmannian / Quot-of-trivial-sheaf functor \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\)]
2084	  \label{def:grassmannian_functor}
2085	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor}
2086	  \uses{def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:quot_functor, def:gr_rankQuotient,
2087	    lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
2088	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
2089	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
2090	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L557-L564).
2091	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2092	  % "represents the
2093	  %  contravariant functor
2094	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
2095	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
2096	  %  set of all equivalence classes
2097	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
2098	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$."
2099	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2).}
2100	  The \emph{Grassmannian functor}
2101	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d) = \Quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}
2102	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/\mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) is the contravariant functor
2103	  from schemes to sets defined as follows. For a scheme \(T\), a \emph{family of
2104	  rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} is a pair
2105	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a locally free
2106	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) of rank \(d\) together with a surjective
2107	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-linear homomorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
2108	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\). Two such families
2109	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) and \(\langle \mathcal{F}', q' \rangle\) are
2110	  \emph{equivalent} if there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F}
2111	  \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with \(f \circ q = q'\); equivalently
2112	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\) as subsheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). Set
2113	  \[
2114	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)
2115	      \;=\; \bigl\{\, \text{equivalence classes } \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle
2116	        \text{ of rank-}d\text{ quotients of } \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \,\bigr\}.
2117	  \]
2118	  For a morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\), the map
2119	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(\varphi)\) sends \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\)
2120	  to the pulled-back quotient \(\langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{F},\, \varphi^{*}q
2121	  \rangle\); this is well-defined on equivalence classes because pullback is right
2122	  exact and preserves local freeness of rank \(d\). (As \(\mathcal{F}\) is locally
2123	  free of rank \(d\), surjectivity is preserved and the support is automatically
2124	  proper -- all of \(T'\) -- so this is the special case
2125	  \(E = \mathcal{O}^{r}\), \(X = S = \operatorname{base}\) of the general Quot
2126	  functor of \cref{def:quot_functor}.)
2127	\end{definition}
2128	
2129	\section{The universal property}
2130	\label{sec:grquot_universal}
2131	
2132	The representability theorem \cref{thm:grassmannian_universal_property} has two halves:
2133	the forward map \(\varphi \mapsto \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u\rangle\)
2134	and its inverse, which manufactures a morphism \(T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) out of a rank-\(d\)
2135	quotient \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) on \(T\). The forward map needs the tautological
2136	quotient to actually be a quotient -- that \(u\) is an epimorphism
2137	(\cref{lem:tautologicalQuotient_epi}). The inverse is built Zariski-locally and glued: for
2138	each size-\(d\) subset \(I\) one isolates the open locus \(T_I \subseteq T\) where the
2139	\(I\)-indexed columns of \(q\) form a basis of \(\mathcal{F}\), classifies \(T_I \to U^I\)
2140	there, and glues over the cover \(\{T_I\}\). The blocks of this section supply the four
2141	ingredients of that inverse: the iso-locus of a sheaf morphism (\cref{def:isoLocus}) and
2142	the fact that a morphism becomes invertible after restriction to its iso-locus
2143	(\cref{lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}); the chart locus \(T_I\) (\cref{def:chartLocus})
2144	and the proof that the chart loci cover \(T\) (\cref{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover}); and the
2145	glued classifying morphism itself (\cref{def:grPointOfRankQuotient}).
2146	
2147	\begin{lemma}[The tautological quotient is an epimorphism]
2148	  \label{lem:tautologicalQuotient_epi}
2149	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.tautologicalQuotient_epi}
2150	  \uses{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi, def:tautological_quotient}
2151	  The tautological quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r}
2152	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}) is an epimorphism of
2153	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}\)-modules.
2154	\end{lemma}
2155	\begin{proof}
2156	  \uses{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi, def:tautological_quotient}
2157	  Being an epimorphism of sheaves of modules is local on the base: a morphism is epi
2158	  precisely when its restriction to every member of an open cover is epi, since the
2159	  cokernel is computed chartwise and a sheaf vanishing on a cover vanishes. The charts
2160	  \(\{U^I\}\) cover \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), and over \(U^I\) the tautological quotient,
2161	  transported through the trivialisation \(\mathcal{U}|_{U^I} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
2162	  of \cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}, is the chart quotient \(u^I :
2163	  \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\) (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}).
2164	  Each \(u^I\) is a split epimorphism: its \(I\)-minor is the identity \(1_{d\times d}\),
2165	  so the coordinate inclusion \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \hookrightarrow
2166	  \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) on the \(I\)-columns is a section of \(u^I\), whence \(u^I\) is
2167	  epi (\cref{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi}). As \(u^I\) is epi on every chart, \(u\) is epi.
2168	\end{proof}
2169	
2170	\begin{definition}[The universal quotient sheaf restricts to the free sheaf on a chart]
2171	  \label{def:gr_universalQuotient_restrictionIso}
2172	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalQuotient_restrictionIso}
2173	  \uses{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:glueRestrictionIso,
2174	    def:gr_bundleTransitionData, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_mul}
2175	  For each chart index \(I\), the restriction of the universal quotient sheaf
2176	  \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}) to the \(I\)-chart along the
2177	  glue-data immersion \(\iota_I : U^I \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is canonically isomorphic to
2178	  the free rank-\(d\) sheaf,
2179	  \[
2180	    \phi_I \;:\; \iota_I^{*}\mathcal{U}
2181	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}.
2182	  \]
2183	  It is the instantiation, at the Grassmannian glue data (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) and
2184	  the bundle-transition cocycle \((g_{I,J})\) (\cref{def:gr_bundleTransitionData},
2185	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id}, \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_mul}), of the descent
2186	  restriction isomorphism (\cref{def:glueRestrictionIso}); its underlying morphism is the
2187	  adjoint transpose of the \(I\)-th descent-equaliser projection, an isomorphism because
2188	  the descent comparison morphism is invertible.
2189	\end{definition}
2190	
2191	\begin{lemma}[The universal quotient sheaf is locally free of rank \(d\)]
2192	  \label{lem:gr_universalQuotient_isLocallyFreeOfRank}
2193	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalQuotient_isLocallyFreeOfRank}
2194	  \uses{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:gr_universalQuotient_restrictionIso,
2195	    def:gr_glued_scheme, def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:modules_pullbackComp,
2196	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
2197	  The universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}) is
2198	  locally free of rank \(d\) (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}).
2199	\end{lemma}
2200	\begin{proof}
2201	  \uses{def:gr_universalQuotient_restrictionIso, def:modules_pullbackComp,
2202	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
2203	  The chart images \(\{\iota_I(U^I)\}\) form an open cover of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), since
2204	  the glue-data immersions are jointly surjective (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}). Over each
2205	  member, factor the immersion as \(\iota_I = e_I \circ j_I\) through the isomorphism
2206	  \(e_I : U^I \xrightarrow{\sim} \iota_I(U^I)\) onto its open image followed by the open
2207	  inclusion \(j_I\); transporting the chart restriction isomorphism
2208	  (\cref{def:gr_universalQuotient_restrictionIso}) across this factorisation -- inverting
2209	  the parametrisation \(e_I\) through the pullback pseudofunctor comparison
2210	  (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}) and the free-pullback comparison
2211	  (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) -- exhibits the restriction
2212	  \(j_I^{*}\mathcal{U} \cong \mathcal{O}_{\iota_I(U^I)}^{\,d}\) as free of rank \(d\). As
2213	  the \(\iota_I(U^I)\) cover \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), \(\mathcal{U}\) is locally free of
2214	  rank \(d\).
2215	\end{proof}

2217	\begin{definition}[The tautological point as a rank-\(d\) quotient]
2218	  \label{def:gr_tautologicalRankQuotient}
2219	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.tautologicalRankQuotient}
2220	  \uses{def:gr_rankQuotient, def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:tautological_quotient,
2221	    lem:tautologicalQuotient_epi, lem:gr_universalQuotient_isLocallyFreeOfRank}
2222	  The tautological pair \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle\) on \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) itself,
2223	  packaged as a rank-\(d\) quotient of \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r}\)
2224	  (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}): its sheaf is the universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\)
2225	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}), its quotient map the tautological quotient
2226	  \(u\) (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}), which is an epimorphism
2227	  (\cref{lem:tautologicalQuotient_epi}) onto a sheaf locally free of rank \(d\)
2228	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalQuotient_isLocallyFreeOfRank}). Pulling it back along a morphism
2229	  \(\psi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) realises the forward direction of the universal
2230	  property.
2231	\end{definition}

2233	\begin{definition}[Iso-locus of a morphism of sheaves of modules]
2234	  \label{def:isoLocus}
2235	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isoLocus}
2236	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) a morphism of sheaves
2237	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The \emph{iso-locus} \(\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi) \subseteq X\)
2238	  is the open subscheme
2239	  \[
2240	    \mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)
2241	      \;=\; \bigsqcup\,\bigl\{\, U \subseteq X \text{ open} \;:\;
2242	        \varphi|_U := (\iota_U^{*}\varphi) \text{ is an isomorphism} \,\bigr\},
2243	  \]
2244	  the supremum (union) of all opens \(U\) over which the pullback \(\iota_U^{*}\varphi\) of
2245	  \(\varphi\) to the open subscheme \(U\) is an isomorphism of sheaves of
2246	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules. Equivalently, a point \(t \in X\) lies in
2247	  \(\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)\) iff \(\varphi\) restricts to an isomorphism over some open
2248	  neighbourhood of \(t\). It is the largest open on which \(\varphi\) is invertible; Mathlib
2249	  has no iso-locus primitive for morphisms of sheaves of modules, so it is set up directly
2250	  as this supremum.
2251	\end{definition}
2252	
2253	\begin{lemma}[Stalk-wise iso criterion]
2254	  \label{lem:isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso_mathlib}
2255	  \lean{TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso}
2256	  \mathlibok
2257	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
2258	  Let \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\) be sheaves on a topological space \(X\) valued in a
2259	  concrete category whose forgetful functor reflects isomorphisms and preserves limits and
2260	  filtered colimits, and let \(f : \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G}\) be a morphism. If the
2261	  induced map on stalks \(f_t : \mathcal{F}_t \to \mathcal{G}_t\) is an isomorphism for every
2262	  point \(t \in X\), then \(f\) is an isomorphism.
2263	\end{lemma}
2264	
2265	\begin{lemma}[A morphism is invertible over its iso-locus]
2266	  \label{lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}
2267	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}
2268	  \uses{def:isoLocus, lem:isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso_mathlib}
2269	  For a morphism \(\varphi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) of sheaves of
2270	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, the pullback of \(\varphi\) to the iso-locus
2271	  (\cref{def:isoLocus}),
2272	  \[
2273	    (\iota_{\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)})^{*}\varphi
2274	      \;:\;
2275	    (\iota_{\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)})^{*}\mathcal{M}
2276	      \;\longrightarrow\;
2277	    (\iota_{\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)})^{*}\mathcal{N},
2278	  \]
2279	  is an isomorphism.
2280	\end{lemma}
2281	\begin{proof}
2282	  \uses{def:isoLocus, lem:isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso_mathlib}
2283	  Being an isomorphism is a stalk-local property: by the stalk-wise criterion
2284	  (\cref{lem:isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso_mathlib}) it suffices to show that the pulled-back
2285	  morphism induces an isomorphism on the stalk at every point \(t \in
2286	  \mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)\). By the definition of the iso-locus as a union of opens on
2287	  which \(\varphi\) restricts to an isomorphism (\cref{def:isoLocus}), each such \(t\) has an
2288	  open neighbourhood \(U \subseteq \mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)\) with \(\iota_U^{*}\varphi\) an
2289	  isomorphism; restriction of an isomorphism is an isomorphism, and pullback commutes with
2290	  taking stalks, so the stalk of the morphism at \(t\) coincides with the stalk of
2291	  \(\iota_U^{*}\varphi\) at \(t\), which is invertible. Hence every stalk map is an
2292	  isomorphism, and the criterion concludes that the global pullback over
2293	  \(\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism.
2294	\end{proof}
2295	
2296	\begin{definition}[Chart composite of a rank-\(d\) quotient]
2297	  \label{def:gr_chartComposite}
2298	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartComposite}
2299	  \uses{def:gr_rankQuotient}
2300	  Let \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) be a rank-\(d\) quotient of
2301	  \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\) (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}) and \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) a
2302	  size-\(d\) subset, enumerated in increasing order by the order isomorphism
2303	  \(\{1,\dots,d\} \xrightarrow{\sim} I\). The \emph{chart composite} is the morphism of
2304	  sheaves of modules
2305	  \[
2306	    s_I \circ q \;:\; \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}
2307	      \overset{\ s_I\ }{\hookrightarrow} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
2308	      \xrightarrow{\ q\ } \mathcal{F},
2309	  \]
2310	  the quotient \(q\) precomposed with the coordinate inclusion \(s_I\) of the
2311	  \(I\)-indexed factors of the trivial bundle. Over the locus where it is invertible the
2312	  \(I\)-columns of \(q\) form a basis of \(\mathcal{F}\).
2313	\end{definition}
2314	
2315	\begin{definition}[Chart locus of a rank-\(d\) quotient]
2316	  \label{def:chartLocus}
2317	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartLocus}
2318	  \uses{def:isoLocus, def:gr_chartComposite, def:gr_rankQuotient}
2319	  Let \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) be a rank-\(d\) quotient of
2320	  \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\) on a scheme \(T\) (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}) and \(I \subseteq
2321	  \{1,\dots,r\}\) a size-\(d\) subset. The \emph{chart composite} is the morphism of sheaves
2322	  of modules
2323	  \[
2324	    s_I \circ q
2325	      \;:\;
2326	    \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}
2327	      \overset{\ s_I\ }{\hookrightarrow} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
2328	      \xrightarrow{\ q\ } \mathcal{F},
2329	  \]
2330	  obtained by precomposing the quotient \(q\) with the inclusion \(s_I\) of the
2331	  \(I\)-indexed coordinates of the trivial bundle. The \emph{chart locus}
2332	  \(T_I = \mathrm{chartLocus}(x, I) \subseteq T\) is the iso-locus
2333	  (\cref{def:isoLocus}) of this chart composite:
2334	  \[
2335	    T_I \;=\; \mathrm{isoLocus}(s_I \circ q),
2336	  \]
2337	  the largest open of \(T\) over which the \(I\)-columns of \(q\) form a basis of
2338	  \(\mathcal{F}\) -- i.e.\ over which \(s_I \circ q\) is an isomorphism. Over \(T_I\) the
2339	  quotient \(q\) is therefore presented, after trivialising \(\mathcal{F}\) by
2340	  \((s_I \circ q)^{-1}\), by a \(d \times r\) matrix whose \(I\)-minor is the identity,
2341	  exactly the shape of the universal matrix \(X^I\) realising the chart quotient \(u^I\)
2342	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}).
2343	\end{definition}
2344	
2345	\begin{lemma}[The chart loci cover \(T\)]
2346	  \label{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover}
2347	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartLocus_isOpenCover}
2348	  \uses{def:chartLocus, def:gr_rankQuotient}
2349	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
2350	  % sub-section "Construction by gluing together affine patches" (read from
2351	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L823-L828).
2352	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2353	  % "For any $J\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with $\#(J) =d$, let
2354	  %  $P^I_J = \det(X^I_J) \in \Z[X^I]$
2355	  %  where $X^I_J$ is the $J$ th minor of $X^I$.
2356	  %  Let $U^I_J = \spec \Z[X^I, 1/P^I_J]$
2357	  %  the open subscheme of $U^I$ where $P^I_J$ is invertible. This means
2358	  %  the $d\times d$-matrix $X^I_J$ admits an inverse $(X^I_J)^{-1}$
2359	  %  on $U^I_J$."
2360	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
2361	  For a rank-\(d\) quotient \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)
2362	  on \(T\) (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}), the family of chart loci
2363	  \(\{\,T_I = \mathrm{chartLocus}(x, I)\,\}_{\#(I) = d}\) (\cref{def:chartLocus}) is an open
2364	  cover of \(T\): \(\bigcup_{\#(I)=d} T_I = T\).
2365	\end{lemma}
2366	\begin{proof}
2367	  \uses{def:chartLocus, def:gr_rankQuotient, def:isoLocus, def:gr_matrixEndRect,
2368	    lem:gr_exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect, lem:gr_exists_isUnit_submatrix}
2369	  Each \(T_I\) is open, being an iso-locus (\cref{def:isoLocus}). It remains to show the
2370	  \(T_I\) cover \(T\), i.e.\ that every point \(t \in T\) lies in some \(T_I\). Fix \(t\) and
2371	  choose an \emph{affine} open \(W \ni t\) over which \(\mathcal{F}\) is trivialised,
2372	  \(\mathcal{F}|_W \cong \mathcal{O}_W^{\,d}\) (such \(W\) exists because \(\mathcal{F}\) is
2373	  locally free of rank \(d\)); over \(W\) the quotient \(q\) is presented by a \(d \times r\)
2374	  matrix \(M\) of sections of \(\mathcal{O}_W\), i.e.\ \(q|_W\) is conjugate to
2375	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) (\cref{def:gr_matrixEndRect}).
2376	
2377	  \emph{Global splitting over \(W\).} Because \(q\) is an epimorphism
2378	  (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}), so is the presenting homomorphism
2379	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) of free sheaves on the affine \(W\). Over an affine base such
2380	  an epimorphism of free sheaves splits -- free modules are projective, so the epimorphism
2381	  admits a section -- and reading the section off as a matrix yields a right inverse
2382	  \(G \in \operatorname{Mat}_{r\times d}(\Gamma(W,\mathcal{O}_W))\) with \(M \cdot G = 1\)
2383	  (\cref{lem:gr_exists_rightInverse_of_epi_matrixEndRect}). Thus \(q\) is globally split over
2384	  \(W\), with no recourse to stalks or Nakayama propagation.
2385	
2386	  \emph{Choosing the index set at \(t\).} Reduce the identity \(M \cdot G = 1\) modulo the
2387	  maximal ideal of the local ring at \(t\): writing \(\kappa(t)\) for the residue field, the
2388	  images \(\bar M, \bar G\) over \(\kappa(t)\) still satisfy \(\bar M \cdot \bar G = 1\), so
2389	  \(\bar M\) is a right-invertible \(d \times r\) matrix over the field \(\kappa(t)\). A
2390	  right-invertible matrix over a field has an invertible \(d\)-column minor: its columns span
2391	  \(\kappa(t)^{d}\), a spanning family contains a basis, and the corresponding \(d\) columns
2392	  form an invertible \(d \times d\) submatrix (\cref{lem:gr_exists_isUnit_submatrix}). Pick
2393	  such a size-\(d\) index set \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\).
2394	
2395	  \emph{Cutting out the basic open.} Let \(P_I = \det(M_I)\) be the determinant of the
2396	  \(I\)-indexed \(d \times d\) minor of \(M\). By the previous step its residue at \(t\) is
2397	  nonzero, so \(P_I\) is a unit at \(t\); hence \(t\) lies in the basic open
2398	  \(W_{P_I} = \{P_I \ne 0\} \subseteq W\) where \(P_I\) is invertible. On \(W_{P_I}\) the
2399	  minor matrix \(M_I\) is invertible, so the chart composite \(s_I \circ q\) -- conjugate
2400	  there to \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M_I)\) -- restricts to an isomorphism, i.e.\
2401	  \(W_{P_I} \subseteq \mathrm{isoLocus}(s_I \circ q) = T_I\) (\cref{def:chartLocus}).
2402	  Therefore \(t \in T_I\), and the chart loci cover \(T\).
2403	\end{proof}
2404	
2405	\begin{definition}[Chart matrix of a rank-\(d\) quotient]
2406	  \label{def:gr_chartMatrix}
2407	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartMatrix}
2408	  \uses{def:gr_chartComposite, def:chartLocus, lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map,
2409	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:gr_presentedMatrix}
2410	  Over the chart locus \(T_I = \mathrm{chartLocus}(x, I)\) (\cref{def:chartLocus}) the chart
2411	  composite \(s_I \circ q\) pulls back to an isomorphism
2412	  (\cref{lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}). Trivialising \(\mathcal{F}|_{T_I}\) by its
2413	  inverse presents the restricted quotient as the conjugated free-sheaf homomorphism
2414	  \[
2415	    Q_r^{-1} \circ \iota_{T_I}^{*}q \circ (\iota_{T_I}^{*}(s_I \circ q))^{-1} \circ Q_d
2416	      \;:\; \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,r} \longrightarrow \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,d},
2417	  \]
2418	  with \(Q_r, Q_d\) the free-pullback comparisons (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}). The
2419	  \emph{chart matrix} \(M^I = \mathrm{chartMatrix}(x, I) \in
2420	  \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(T_I, \mathcal{O}_{T_I}))\) reads off this
2421	  homomorphism entrywise through the section-extraction \(\mathrm{unitEndSection}\). It is
2422	  the special case of the presented matrix (\cref{def:gr_presentedMatrix}) along the
2423	  chart-locus inclusion; its \(I\)-minor is the identity \(1_{d\times d}\).
2424	\end{definition}
2425	
2426	\begin{definition}[Chart morphism of a rank-\(d\) quotient]
2427	  \label{def:gr_chartMorphism}
2428	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartMorphism}
2429	  \uses{def:gr_chartMatrix, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix}
2430	  The \emph{chart morphism} \(\varphi_I : T_I \to U^I\) into the affine chart
2431	  \(U^I = \Spec \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) is the morphism classified,
2432	  through the \(\Gamma\)--\(\Spec\) adjunction, by the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism
2433	  \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to \Gamma(T_I, \mathcal{O}_{T_I})\) sending each variable
2434	  \(x^I_{p,q}\) of the universal matrix \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}) to the
2435	  corresponding entry of the chart matrix \(M^I\) (\cref{def:gr_chartMatrix}). By
2436	  construction \(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\), and hence \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I = q|_{T_I}\) in the
2437	  \(I\)-trivialisation.
2438	\end{definition}
2439	
2440	\subsection{Overlap compatibility of the chart morphisms}
2441	
2442	On the intersection \(P = T_I \times_T T_J\) of two chart loci both chart composites pull
2443	back to isomorphisms, so the pulled-back quotient is presented by both chart matrices. The
2444	blocks below assemble the transports comparing the two presentations and proving the two
2445	chart morphisms agree into the glued scheme: invertibility along a composite
2446	(\cref{lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp}), the presented matrix along a composite
2447	(\cref{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_comp}) and under an equality of base morphisms
2448	(\cref{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_congr}), the chart matrix as a presented matrix
2449	(\cref{lem:gr_chartMatrix_eq_presentedMatrix}), its own minor
2450	(\cref{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_submatrix_self}), the realisation
2451	\(\mathrm{aeval}(M^I)(X^I) = M^I\) (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_presentedMatrix}), the
2452	image-matrix transport (\cref{lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_ringHom}), chart-morphism naturality
2453	(\cref{lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism}), the glue-condition identity
2454	(\cref{lem:gr_chart_point_eq}), and finally the overlap compatibility itself
2455	(\cref{lem:gr_chartMorphism_glue_compat}).
2456	
2457	\begin{lemma}[Pullback-invertibility persists along precomposition]
2458	  \label{lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp}
2459	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isIso_pullback_map_comp}
2460	  \uses{def:modules_pullbackComp}
2461	  For composable scheme morphisms \(p : W \to V\), \(a : V \to X\) and a morphism
2462	  \(c : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules: if the
2463	  pullback \(a^{*}c\) is an isomorphism, so is \((p \circ a)^{*}c\).
2464	\end{lemma}
2465	\begin{proof}
2466	  \uses{def:modules_pullbackComp}
2467	  Functoriality of pullback makes \(p^{*}(a^{*}c)\) an isomorphism; the pseudofunctor
2468	  comparison \((p \circ a)^{*} \cong p^{*}a^{*}\) (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp})
2469	  identifies \((p \circ a)^{*}c\) with \(p^{*}(a^{*}c)\), which is invertible.
2470	\end{proof}
2471	
2472	\begin{lemma}[The presented matrix depends only on the base morphism]
2473	  \label{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_congr}
2474	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.presentedMatrix_congr}
2475	  \uses{def:gr_presentedMatrix}
2476	  If \(j = j'\) as morphisms \(P \to T\), then the presented matrices along \(j\) and \(j'\)
2477	  agree, \(\mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, I) = \mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j', I)\); the
2478	  choice of invertibility witness for the pulled-back chart composite is irrelevant.
2479	\end{lemma}
2480	\begin{proof}
2481	  \uses{def:gr_presentedMatrix}
2482	  Substitute the equality \(j = j'\); the invertibility hypotheses are propositions, hence
2483	  proof-irrelevant, and the two sides become definitionally equal.
2484	\end{proof}
2485	
2486	\begin{lemma}[The chart matrix is the presented matrix along the locus inclusion]
2487	  \label{lem:gr_chartMatrix_eq_presentedMatrix}
2488	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartMatrix_eq_presentedMatrix}
2489	  \uses{def:gr_chartMatrix, def:gr_presentedMatrix}
2490	  The chart matrix \(M^I = \mathrm{chartMatrix}(x, I)\) (\cref{def:gr_chartMatrix}) equals
2491	  the presented matrix (\cref{def:gr_presentedMatrix}) of \(x\) along the chart-locus
2492	  inclusion \(\iota_{T_I} : T_I \hookrightarrow T\):
2493	  \(\mathrm{chartMatrix}(x, I) = \mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, \iota_{T_I}, I)\).
2494	\end{lemma}
2495	\begin{proof}
2496	  \uses{def:gr_chartMatrix, def:gr_presentedMatrix}
2497	  Both sides are the same \(\mathrm{unitEndSection}\)-matrix of the same conjugated
2498	  free-sheaf homomorphism; the equality is definitional.
2499	\end{proof}
2500	
2501	\begin{lemma}[Transport of the presented matrix along a composite]
2502	  \label{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_comp}
2503	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.presentedMatrix_comp}
2504	  \uses{def:gr_presentedMatrix, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix,
2505	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}
2506	  Along composable \(p : W \to V\), \(a : V \to T\) on which the \(I\)-chart composite
2507	  pulls back to isomorphisms, the presented matrix after pulling back along \(p \circ a\)
2508	  is the entrywise restriction along \(p\) of the presented matrix after pulling back
2509	  along \(a\):
2510	  \[
2511	    \mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, p \circ a, I)
2512	      \;=\; \bigl(\mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, a, I)\bigr)\big|_{p},
2513	  \]
2514	  the matrix obtained by applying the restriction ring map \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{O}_V) \to
2515	  \Gamma(W, \mathcal{O}_W)\) of \(p\) to every entry.
2516	\end{lemma}
2517	\begin{proof}
2518	  \uses{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}
2519	  By injectivity of the rectangular matrix presentation
2520	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}) it suffices to compare after applying
2521	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\). The presentation identity
2522	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix}) expresses both sides as conjugated
2523	  pulled-back homomorphisms; the conjugation distributes over the composite \(p \circ a\)
2524	  through the pullback pseudofunctor comparisons, and the entrywise restriction along
2525	  \(p\) is exactly the pullback along \(p\) of the \(a\)-level homomorphism. Comparing the
2526	  two yields the claimed equality.
2527	\end{proof}
2528	
2529	\begin{lemma}[The own-minor of the presented matrix is the identity]
2530	  \label{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_submatrix_self}
2531	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.presentedMatrix_submatrix_self}
2532	  \uses{def:gr_presentedMatrix, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor,
2533	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}
2534	  Along any \(j\) on which the \(I\)-chart composite pulls back to an isomorphism, the
2535	  \(I\)-minor of the presented matrix is the identity:
2536	  \(\mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, I)_{\bullet,\, I} = 1_{d\times d}\).
2537	\end{lemma}
2538	\begin{proof}
2539	  \uses{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one,
2540	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}
2541	  By injectivity (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_injective}) compare after
2542	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\). The minor presentation identity
2543	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor}) at \(J = I\) presents the
2544	  \(I\)-chart composite against its own inverse, which is the identity morphism, i.e.\
2545	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(1_{d\times d})\) (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_one}).
2546	\end{proof}

2548	\begin{lemma}[The classifying map realises the universal matrix as the presented matrix]
2549	  \label{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_presentedMatrix}
2550	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMatrix_map_presentedMatrix}
2551	  \uses{def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_presentedMatrix,
2552	    lem:gr_presentedMatrix_submatrix_self}
2553	  Applying entrywise the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra map \(\mathrm{aeval}(M)\) -- which sends
2554	  the free variables of \(X^I\) to the complementary entries of
2555	  \(M = \mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, j, I)\) -- to the universal matrix \(X^I\)
2556	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}) returns \(M\):
2557	  \(\mathrm{aeval}(M)(X^I) = M\).
2558	\end{lemma}
2559	\begin{proof}
2560	  \uses{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_submatrix_self}
2561	  Entrywise. On a complementary (free) entry \(x^I_{p,q}\) with \(q \notin I\),
2562	  \(\mathrm{aeval}(M)\) returns the corresponding entry of \(M\) by the variable-evaluation
2563	  rule. On an \(I\)-column entry the universal matrix has the identity minor, so the value
2564	  there is the corresponding entry of \(1_{d\times d}\), which matches the \(I\)-minor of
2565	  \(M\) -- the identity by \cref{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_submatrix_self}.
2566	\end{proof}

2568	\begin{lemma}[Transport of the image matrix along a ring homomorphism]
2569	  \label{lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_ringHom}
2570	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.imageMatrix_map_ringHom}
2571	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, def:gr_universal_matrix, lem:gr_minorDet_unit,
2572	    lem:gr_map_nonsing_inv}
2573	  For size-\(d\) subsets \(I, J\) and any ring homomorphism
2574	  \(\mathrm{incl} : R^I_J \to D\) out of the localised overlap ring whose composite with
2575	  the structure map \(R^I \to R^I_J\) equals \(g\), pushing the image matrix
2576	  \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) forward along \(\mathrm{incl}\) yields \(\bigl((g_{*}X^I)_J\bigr)^{-1}
2577	  (g_{*}X^I)\), where \(g_{*}X^I\) is \(X^I\) base-changed along \(g\):
2578	  \[
2579	    \mathrm{incl}_{*}\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\bigr)
2580	      \;=\; \bigl((g_{*}X^I)_J\bigr)^{-1}\, (g_{*}X^I).
2581	  \]
2582	\end{lemma}
2583	\begin{proof}
2584	  \uses{lem:gr_minorDet_unit, lem:gr_map_nonsing_inv}
2585	  The image matrix is the product of the Cramer inverse \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\)
2586	  (\cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}) with \(X^I\); ring homomorphisms commute with matrix
2587	  multiplication, with the submatrix (minor) operation, and -- because the minor
2588	  determinant \(P^I_J\) is a unit (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}) -- with the nonsingular
2589	  inverse (\cref{lem:gr_map_nonsing_inv}). Pushing each factor forward along
2590	  \(\mathrm{incl}\) and rewriting the structure-map composite as \(g\) gives the stated
2591	  base-changed expression.
2592	\end{proof}
2593	
2594	\begin{lemma}[Naturality of the chart morphism]
2595	  \label{lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism}
2596	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.comp_chartMorphism}
2597	  \uses{def:gr_chartMorphism, lem:gr_chartMatrix_eq_presentedMatrix,
2598	    lem:gr_presentedMatrix_comp}
2599	  For \(p : W \to T_I\) on which the pulled-back \(I\)-chart composite is invertible, the
2600	  composite \(p \circ \varphi_I : W \to U^I\) is the morphism classified by the matrix
2601	  presenting the quotient along \(p \circ \iota_{T_I}\) -- i.e.\ classified by
2602	  \(\mathrm{presentedMatrix}(x, p \circ \iota_{T_I}, I)\).
2603	\end{lemma}
2604	\begin{proof}
2605	  \uses{lem:gr_chartMatrix_eq_presentedMatrix, lem:gr_presentedMatrix_comp}
2606	  Naturality of the \(\Gamma\)--\(\Spec\) classifying map turns \(p \circ \varphi_I\) into
2607	  the morphism classified by the \(p\)-restriction of the chart matrix. The chart matrix is
2608	  the presented matrix along \(\iota_{T_I}\) (\cref{lem:gr_chartMatrix_eq_presentedMatrix}),
2609	  and its \(p\)-restriction is the presented matrix along \(p \circ \iota_{T_I}\)
2610	  (\cref{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_comp}); the two classifying maps therefore coincide.
2611	\end{proof}

2613	\begin{lemma}[Chart-point identity over a commutative ring]
2614	  \label{lem:gr_chart_point_eq}
2615	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chart_point_eq}
2616	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_glued_scheme, lem:gr_chartTransition_comp_chartIncl}
2617	  Let \(K\) be a commutative ring and \(f : R^I \to K\) a ring map under which the minor
2618	  \(P^I_J\) is a unit. Then the transported classifying map
2619	  \(\mathrm{lift}(f) \circ \tilde\theta_{I,J}\) presents the same \(K\)-point through chart
2620	  \(J\) as \(f\) does through chart \(I\):
2621	  \[
2622	    \Spec(\mathrm{lift}(f) \circ \tilde\theta_{I,J}) \circ \iota_J
2623	      \;=\; \Spec(f) \circ \iota_I.
2624	  \]
2625	\end{lemma}
2626	\begin{proof}
2627	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_glued_scheme, lem:gr_chartTransition_comp_chartIncl}
2628	  Factor \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) as the chart transition followed by the chart inclusion
2629	  (\cref{lem:gr_chartTransition_comp_chartIncl}); the glue condition of the glue data
2630	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) makes the transition square commute, and the localisation
2631	  lift recovers \(f\) after the chart inclusion. Assembling the two reductions identifies
2632	  the two composites into the glued scheme.
2633	\end{proof}

2635	\begin{lemma}[Overlap compatibility of the chart morphisms]
2636	  \label{lem:gr_chartMorphism_glue_compat}
2637	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartMorphism_glue_compat}
2638	  \uses{def:gr_chartMorphism, def:gr_glued_scheme, lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp,
2639	    lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism, lem:gr_presentedMatrix_changeOfBasis,
2640	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_presentedMatrix, lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_ringHom,
2641	    lem:gr_chart_point_eq, lem:gr_isUnit_of_isIso_matrixEndRect,
2642	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor}
2643	  On the intersection \(P = T_I \times_T T_J\) the two composites of the chart morphisms
2644	  with the glue-data immersions agree:
2645	  \[
2646	    \mathrm{pr}_1 \circ (\varphi_I \circ \iota_I)
2647	      \;=\; \mathrm{pr}_2 \circ (\varphi_J \circ \iota_J).
2648	  \]
2649	  This is the gluing hypothesis from which \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(x)\) is
2650	  assembled.
2651	\end{lemma}
2652	\begin{proof}
2653	  \uses{lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp, lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism,
2654	    lem:gr_presentedMatrix_changeOfBasis, lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_presentedMatrix,
2655	    lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_ringHom, lem:gr_chart_point_eq,
2656	    lem:gr_isUnit_of_isIso_matrixEndRect, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor}
2657	  On \(P\) both chart composites pull back to isomorphisms
2658	  (\cref{lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp}), so the pulled-back quotient is presented by both
2659	  matrices \(M^I_P\) and \(M^J_P\); through \cref{lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism} the two
2660	  composites are classified by these matrices. The \(J\)-minor of \(M^I_P\) is invertible
2661	  (\cref{lem:gr_isUnit_of_isIso_matrixEndRect},
2662	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_presentedMatrix_minor}), and the change-of-basis identity
2663	  \(M^I_P = (M^I_P)_J \cdot M^J_P\) (\cref{lem:gr_presentedMatrix_changeOfBasis}) --
2664	  realised on the universal matrices via
2665	  \cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_presentedMatrix} and the image-matrix transport
2666	  \cref{lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_ringHom} -- identifies the \(J\)-classifying ring map with
2667	  the localisation-transport \(\mathrm{lift}(\mathrm{aeval}\,M^I_P) \circ
2668	  \tilde\theta_{I,J}\) of the \(I\)-classifying map. The glue-condition identity
2669	  (\cref{lem:gr_chart_point_eq}) then closes the square into the glued scheme.
2670	\end{proof}

2672	\begin{definition}[The morphism induced by a rank-\(d\) quotient]
2673	  \label{def:grPointOfRankQuotient}
2674	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.grPointOfRankQuotient}
2675	  \uses{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover, lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map,
2676	    def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle,
2677	    def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_chartComposite, def:gr_chartMatrix,
2678	    def:gr_chartMorphism, lem:gr_chartMorphism_glue_compat}
2679	  Let \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) be a rank-\(d\) quotient of
2680	  \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\) on a scheme \(T\) (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}). The morphism
2681	  \[
2682	    \mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(x) \;:\; T \;\longrightarrow\; \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)
2683	  \]
2684	  is constructed as follows. Over each chart locus \(T_I = \mathrm{chartLocus}(x, I)\)
2685	  (\cref{def:chartLocus}) the chart composite \(s_I \circ q\) is an isomorphism
2686	  (\cref{lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}), so trivialising \(\mathcal{F}|_{T_I}\) by its
2687	  inverse presents \(q|_{T_I}\) as a \(d \times r\) matrix \(M^I\) of global functions on
2688	  \(T_I\) whose \(I\)-minor is the identity. Its \(d(r-d)\) complementary entries determine a
2689	  unique \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to \Gamma(T_I,
2690	  \mathcal{O}_{T_I})\), \(x^I_{p,q} \mapsto M^I_{p,q}\), hence by the \(\Gamma\)--\(\Spec\)
2691	  adjunction a unique \emph{chart morphism} \(\varphi_I : T_I \to U^I\)
2692	  (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) with \(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\), and thus
2693	  \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I = q|_{T_I}\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}). The chart loci cover \(T\)
2694	  (\cref{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover}), and on each overlap \(T_I \cap T_J\) the two
2695	  presenting matrices are change-of-basis related by the invertible \(I\)-columns minor,
2696	  \(M^J = (M^I_J)^{-1} M^I = \varphi_I^{*}\theta_{I,J}(X^J)\)
2697	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}); hence the composites \(T_I \xrightarrow{\varphi_I} U^I
2698	  \hookrightarrow \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) with the chart immersions agree on overlaps after the
2699	  transition \(\theta_{I,J}\). By the cocycle condition (\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}) these
2700	  chart-local morphisms glue to a single morphism \(T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\)
2701	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}); this glued morphism is
2702	  \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(x)\), the local-to-global inverse of the universal
2703	  property: pulling the tautological quotient back along it recovers \(\langle \mathcal{F},
2704	  q\rangle\) up to equivalence.
2705	\end{definition}

2707	The two blocks below carry the chart data of a quotient through a base change
2708	\(\psi : T' \to T\); they are the bridge underlying the two inverse laws of the
2709	representability theorem.

324	      ((biproduct.isoCoproduct

326	        biproduct.matrix (fun (i' : Fin r) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd
327	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom (universalMatrix d r I hI p i'))) ≫
328	        (biproduct.isoCoproduct
329	            (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom)
330	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree k
331	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, SheafOfModules.ιFree, biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv]
332	  erw [Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
333	  rw [biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.isoCoproduct_hom]
334	  have h1 : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv) (1 : A) = 1 := map_one _
335	  have h0 : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv) (0 : A) = 0 := map_zero _
336	  have hsub := universalMatrix_submatrix_self d r I hI
337	  have lift_eq :
338	      (biproduct.lift fun p : Fin d => scalarEnd
339	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom (universalMatrix d r I hI p (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k)))))
340	        = biproduct.ι (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) k := by
341	    refine biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_)
342	    rw [biproduct.lift_π]
343	    have hentry : universalMatrix d r I hI p (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k))
344	        = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) A) p k :=
345	      congrFun (congrFun hsub p) k
346	    rw [hentry, Matrix.one_apply]
347	    by_cases hpk : p = k
348	    · rw [if_pos hpk, h1, scalarEnd_one, hpk, biproduct.ι_π_self]
349	    · rw [if_neg hpk, h0, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm hpk)]
350	  rw [lift_eq, biproduct.ι_desc]
351	  rfl

353	/-- **The chart quotient is an epimorphism** (`lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`): `u^I` is split
354	by the coordinate inclusion `s_I` of the `I`-indexed columns, hence is a (split) epimorphism
355	of sheaves of modules. -/
356	lemma chartQuotientMap_epi (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
357	    Epi (chartQuotientMap d r I hI) := by
358	  have hsplit : SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
359	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
360	      = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) := by
361	    refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d)) _ _ (fun k => ?_)
362	    have key : SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) k ≫
363	          (SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫
364	            chartQuotientMap d r I hI)
365	        = SheafOfModules.ιFree k :=
366	      (SheafOfModules.ιFree_freeMap_assoc (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) k
367	        (chartQuotientMap d r I hI)).trans (chartQuotientMap_ιFree d r I hI k)
368	    exact key.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm
369	  exact (IsSplitEpi.mk' ⟨_, hsplit⟩).epi

375	/-! ## The GL_d bundle transition cocycle
376	
377	The universal quotient `U` is glued from the per-chart free rank-`d` sheaves `O_{U^I}^d`
378	along the bundle transitions `g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)⁻¹`, realised as matrix automorphisms via
379	`matrixToFreeIso` and conjugated to the overlap pullbacks by `pullbackFreeIso`. This section
380	constructs `bundleTransition` and proves its self-identity (C1); the triple-overlap
381	multiplicativity (C2) is the matrix cocycle of `lem:gr_cocycle` transported to the common
382	overlap by `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`/`glueData_bridge_*`. -/
383	
384	/-- The Cramer inverse of the self-minor `X^I_I` is the identity: since `X^I_I = 1`
385	(`universalMatrix_submatrix_self`) its inverse is `1`. Project-local; underlies C1. -/
386	lemma universalMinorInv_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
387	    universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
388	  have hmin : universalMinor d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
389	    rw [universalMinor, universalMatrix_submatrix_self, Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)]
390	  rw [universalMinorInv, hmin, inv_one]
391	
392	/-- The injected Cramer inverse and minor matrices over the overlap structure sheaf are
393	mutually inverse — the `GL_d` invertibility hypotheses for `matrixToFreeIso`. Project-local. -/
394	private lemma bundleMatrix_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
395	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
396	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
397	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
398	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) *
399	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
400	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
401	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 ∧
402	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
403	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
404	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) *
405	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
406	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
407	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 := by
408	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
409	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, map_one]
410	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2, map_one]
411	
412	/-- The **bundle transition** `g_{I,J}` (`def:gr_bundleTransition`): the isomorphism of
413	sheaves of modules on the overlap `U^I_J` induced by the invertible matrix
414	`(X^I_J)⁻¹ = universalMinorInv d r I J`. It identifies the pullback of `O_{U^I}^d` along
415	`f_{IJ}` with the pullback of `O_{U^J}^d` along `t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI}`, by conjugating the
416	matrix automorphism `matrixToFreeIso (X^I_J)⁻¹` (built like `chartQuotientMap`) by the
417	free-pullback comparisons `pullbackFreeIso`. -/
418	noncomputable def bundleTransition (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r))
419	    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
420	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)).obj
421	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) ≅
422	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)).obj
423	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
424	  Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (Fin d) ≪≫
425	    matrixToFreeIso
426	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
427	          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
428	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ))
429	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
430	          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
431	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ))
432	      (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1
433	      (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2 ≪≫
434	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso
435	      (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI) (Fin d)).symm
436	
437	/-- **Self-identity of the bundle transition (C1)** (`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id`): on the
438	diagonal overlap `U^I_I` (where `t_{II} = 𝟙`) the bundle transition is the identity, in the
439	form required by the gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. The matrix part is the identity
440	since `(X^I_I)⁻¹ = 1` (`universalMinorInv_self`), so `matrixEnd 1 = 𝟙` (`matrixEnd_one`); the
441	two free-pullback comparisons then cancel into the `eqToIso` transport.
442	
443	Resource note (iter-060): the former `set_option maxHeartbeats 1000000 in` override is
444	removed and the proof rebuilt as a *leaner term* that the kernel checks within the default
445	budget (the earlier `.hom`-level cast chain hit a `(kernel) deterministic timeout` at default
446	heartbeats and an OOM ceiling on cold builds at `1000000`). The new term works at the **iso
447	level**: the matrix automorphism is collapsed to `Iso.refl` in the lightweight single-overlap
448	context (`hB`, free sheaves only — no pullback), and the two free-pullback comparisons cancel
449	through the *generic* lemma `pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso` (proved by `subst` on
450	variable morphisms), so the kernel never whnfs the concrete immersions `chartIncl` /
451	`chartTransition`. -/
452	theorem bundleTransition_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
453	    bundleTransition d r I I hI hI
454	      = eqToIso (congrArg
455	          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
456	            (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)))
457	          (show chartIncl d r I I hI hI
458	              = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI from by
459	            rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp])) := by
460	  have hφ : chartIncl d r I I hI hI
461	      = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI := by
462	    rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp]
463	  -- The matrix automorphism is the identity iso: `(X^I_I)⁻¹` injects to the identity matrix,
464	  -- so its `matrixEnd` is `𝟙`. Proved here over the single overlap chart (no pullback types).
465	  have hB : matrixToFreeIso
466	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
467	            (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
468	          (universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI))
469	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
470	            (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
471	          (universalMinor d r I I hI hI))
472	        (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I I hI hI).1
473	        (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I I hI hI).2
474	      = Iso.refl _ := by
475	    apply Iso.ext
476	    rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, Iso.refl_hom, universalMinorInv_self, map_one, matrixEnd_one]
477	  -- Unfold the transition, collapse the identity matrix factor (`erw` to bridge the
478	  -- `chartOverlap`/`Spec` defeq on the inferred base scheme), and cancel the comparisons.
479	  simp only [bundleTransition]
480	  erw [hB, Iso.refl_trans]
481	  exact Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso hφ (Fin d)
482	
483	/-- The bundle transition data packaged over the Grassmannian glue datum, ready to feed the
484	gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. Project-local. -/
485	noncomputable def bundleTransitionData (d r : ℕ) :
486	    ∀ (I J : (theGlueData d r).J),
487	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).f I J)).obj
488	          (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) ≅
489	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).t I J ≫ (theGlueData d r).f J I)).obj
490	          (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
491	  fun I J => bundleTransition d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2

493	/-! ### The matrix-level Cramer-inverse cocycle (L1)

495	The matrix-algebra core of (C2) is the Cramer-inverse cocycle
496	`(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹` over the triple-overlap ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`.
497	Its proof reduces to the image-matrix cocycle `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` of
498	`GrassmannianCells` by taking the `I`-minor. That lemma and the matrix helpers it depends on
499	are `private` in `GrassmannianCells.lean`, so they are reproduced here as project-local
500	helpers (the proofs are verbatim ports of the known-good originals). -/

503	private lemma mul_submatrix_col' {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
504	    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
505	    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
506	  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]

509	private lemma map_nonsing_inv' {n : ℕ} {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S)
510	    (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) (h : IsUnit A.det) :
511	    (A.map f)⁻¹ = A⁻¹.map f := by
512	  have hmul : (A.map f) * (A⁻¹.map f) = 1 := by
513	    rw [← Matrix.map_mul, Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A h, Matrix.map_one f (map_zero f) (map_one f)]
514	  exact Matrix.inv_eq_right_inv hmul

639	/-- **Cramer-inverse cocycle on the triple overlap (L1)** (`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix`):
640	over the triple-overlap ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]` the base-changed Cramer inverses of
641	the localised minors satisfy the multiplicative cocycle identity
642	`(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹`. This is the pure matrix-algebra core of (C2), independent
643	of any sheaf data. Project-local. -/
644	theorem bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r))
645	    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
646	    (universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK).map
647	        ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
648	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
649	      * (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).map
650	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
651	      = (universalMinorInv d r I K hI hK).map
652	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
653	  -- Take the `I`-minor (columns indexed by `I`) of the image-matrix cocycle.
654	  have hcol := congrArg
655	    (fun M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away
656	        (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK)) =>
657	      M.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)))
658	    (cocycle_imageMatrix_eq' d r I J K hI hJ hK)
659	  simp only at hcol
660	  -- LHS of `hcol` is `(X^I_K)⁻¹` over `S_I`.
661	  rw [Matrix.submatrix_map, imageMatrix_submatrix_I] at hcol
662	  -- RHS of `hcol`: push the `I`-minor through the outer map.
663	  rw [Matrix.submatrix_map] at hcol
664	  -- `imageMatrix J K = (X^J_K)⁻¹ * X^J`, so its `I`-minor splits off the inverse factor;
665	  -- the second factor is `X^J` (over `R^J[1/P^J_K]`) restricted to the `I`-columns.
666	  have hsplit : (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).submatrix id
667	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
668	      = universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK *
669	        ((universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
670	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
671	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).submatrix id
672	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) := by
673	    rw [imageMatrix]; exact mul_submatrix_col' _ _ _
674	  rw [hsplit, Matrix.map_mul] at hcol
675	  -- The comp identity `θ_{I,J}` realises the cross-localisation map on `R^J`.
676	  have hχ : ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
677	        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))).comp
678	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
679	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
680	      = (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
681	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom := by
682	    rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap, cocycleΘIJ]
683	    exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp _ _
684	  have e1 : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
685	        ((awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
686	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
687	      = (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).map
688	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
689	    rw [← map_map_eq_of_comp' (universalMatrix d r J hJ)
690	        (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom
691	        (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) _ rfl]
692	    congr 1
693	    exact universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ
694	  -- The base change of `X^J|_I` over `θ_{I,J}` is `(X^I_J)⁻¹` over `S_I`.
695	  have hXJI : (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
696	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
697	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).submatrix id
698	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map
699	          ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
700	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
701	      = (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).map
702	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
703	    rw [Matrix.submatrix_map, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hχ, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, e1,
704	      Matrix.submatrix_map, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
705	  rw [hXJI] at hcol
706	  exact hcol.symm
707	
708	/-! ### L3 transport: `scalarEnd`/`matrixEnd` naturality under pullback
709	
710	The substantive new infrastructure for (C2). The atom is `scalarEnd_pullback`: pulling back a
711	scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` along a scheme morphism `p` is, after the unit-pullback
712	comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, the scalar endomorphism of the base-changed function
713	`p.appTop a`. Its proof transposes the naturality square under the pullback-pushforward
714	adjunction to a `unit`-level identity, which holds by naturality of the comorphism `p.c`. -/
715	
716	/-- The reduced (transposed) form of the scalar-naturality atom: on the unit sheaf,
717	multiplication by `a` followed by the comorphism `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` equals the
718	comorphism followed by the pushforward of multiplication by `p.appTop a`. Project-local
719	helper for `scalarEnd_pullback`. -/
720	lemma unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) (a : Γ(S, ⊤)) :
721	    scalarEnd a ≫ SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
722	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) ≫
723	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map (scalarEnd (p.appTop a)) := by
724	  apply ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).obj
725	    (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)).unitHomEquiv.injective
726	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
727	  -- Both `.val Y` are nested applications (no morphism composite) up to defeq, since
728	  -- `unitHomEquiv (f ≫ p) = sectionsMap p (unitHomEquiv f)` and `sectionsMap`/`unitHomEquiv`
729	  -- are `rfl`/`sectionsMk`-defined; rewrite into that form via `change`.
730	  change (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)).val.app Y
731	        ((scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : S.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
732	      = ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map
733	            (scalarEnd ((Scheme.Hom.appTop p) a))).val.app Y
734	        ((SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)).val.app Y
735	          (1 : S.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
736	  rw [scalarEnd_val_app_one, SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply,
737	    SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply, map_one]
738	  -- Goal: `φ.hom.app Y (a|_Y) = ((pushforward p).map (scalarEnd (p.appTop a))).val.app Y 1`.
739	  -- RHS reduces (defeq, the pushforward's `map`-application is `rfl` + `scalarEnd_val_app_one`)
740	  -- to `T.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (p.appTop a)`; LHS rewrites to the same by
741	  -- naturality of the comorphism `(toRingCatSheafHom p).hom` at `(homOfLE le_top).op : op ⊤ ⟶ Y`.
742	  have hnat := ConcreteCategory.congr_hom
743	    ((Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p).hom.naturality (homOfLE (le_top : Y.unop ≤ ⊤)).op) a
744	  rw [CategoryTheory.comp_apply, CategoryTheory.comp_apply] at hnat
745	  rw [hnat]
746	  -- The RHS pushforward (its `map`-application is `rfl` on sections) evaluates the
747	  -- scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd (p.appTop a)` at `1` over the preimage open; both sides are
748	  -- then `T.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (p.appTop a)` (the `homOfLE`s agree by
749	  -- proof irrelevance, the comorphism by `forget₂`-on-elements), so `scalarEnd_val_app_one` closes.
750	  exact (scalarEnd_val_app_one ((Scheme.Hom.appTop p) a)
751	    (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map p.base).obj (Opposite.unop Y)))).symm
752	
753	/-- **ATOM: scalar endomorphism naturality under pullback** (`lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback`).
754	For `p : T ⟶ S` and `a ∈ Γ(S,⊤)`, pulling back the scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` is,
755	after the unit-pullback comparison `q = pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, the scalar endomorphism of the
756	base-changed function `p.appTop a`:
757	`(pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) ≫ q = q ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a)`.
758	Proved by transposing under the pullback-pushforward adjunction to
759	`unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm`.
760	Project-local — the single irreducible new claim underlying `matrixEnd_pullback`. -/
761	lemma scalarEnd_pullback {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) (a : Γ(S, ⊤)) :
762	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) ≫
763	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
764	      = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) ≫
765	        scalarEnd (p.appTop a) := by
766	  apply (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).homEquiv
767	    (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf) |>.injective
768	  -- `homEquiv_naturality_left` collapses the LHS transpose; the RHS transpose
769	  -- (`homEquiv_naturality_right`) is supplied as a term because positional `rw` cannot match
770	  -- the identical-printing `homEquiv` under the `X.Modules` diamond (memory
771	  -- `grquot-functor-dropped-termmode`).
772	  -- `hq` is the lemma `..._homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit` restated in the `Scheme.Modules`
773	  -- adjunction form (defeq to the `SheafOfModules` form), so `rw`/`congrArg` match syntactically.
774	  have hq : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).homEquiv
775	        (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)
776	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p))
777	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) :=
778	    SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit _
779	  rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left, hq]
780	  refine (unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm p a).trans ?_
781	  exact (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).homEquiv_naturality_right
782	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p))
783	        (scalarEnd (p.appTop a))).trans
784	      (congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map (scalarEnd (p.appTop a))) hq)).symm
785	
786	/-- The action of `matrixEnd M` on the `j`-th free injection: `ιFree j ≫ matrixEnd M`
787	expands as the sum over rows `∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ ιFree k`. Project-local helper
788	for `matrixEnd_pullback`. -/
789	lemma ιFree_matrixEnd {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤))
790	    (j : Fin d) :
791	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) j ≫ matrixEnd M
792	      = ∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) k := by
793	  rw [matrixEnd, SheafOfModules.ιFree]
794	  simp only [SheafOfModules.free]
795	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv, biproduct.isoCoproduct_hom,
796	    ← Category.assoc, Sigma.ι_desc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.lift_desc]
797	  rfl
798	
799	/-- **(a) Matrix endomorphism naturality under pullback** (`lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback`).
800	For `p : T ⟶ S` and `M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S,⊤)`, the pullback of the matrix
801	endomorphism `matrixEnd M` is, after the free-pullback comparison `Q = pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)`,
802	the matrix endomorphism of the base-changed matrix `p.appTop • M` (entrywise comorphism):
803	`(pullback p).map (matrixEnd M) = Q.hom ≫ matrixEnd (p.appTop.mapMatrix M) ≫ Q.inv`.
804	Reduces, on each one-element biproduct component, to the scalar atom `scalarEnd_pullback`.
805	Project-local. -/
806	lemma matrixEnd_pullback {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) {d : ℕ}
807	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
808	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEnd M)
809	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom ≫
810	        matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)).mapMatrix M) ≫
811	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv := by
812	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final p
813	  -- Reduce to the naturality square (cancel the trailing `Q.inv`).
814	  rw [← Category.assoc, Iso.eq_comp_inv]
815	  -- Check the two maps out of the coproduct `(pullback p).obj (free (Fin d))` agree on each
816	  -- free injection `(pullback p).map (ιFree i)` (the cofan of the preserved colimit).
817	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext
818	    (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit (Scheme.Modules.pullback p) _ _
819	      (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d))) _ _ (fun i => ?_)
820	  simp only [cofan_mk_inj, Cofan.mk_pt]
821	  -- `Q.hom` is, by construction of `pullbackFreeIso`, the Mathlib free-pullback comparison.
822	  have hQhom : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
823	      = (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) (Fin d)).hom := rfl
824	  -- The free injection cancels against `Q.hom` into the unit-pullback comparison
825	  -- (`pullbackObjUnitToUnit`), which is where `scalarEnd_pullback` lives.
826	  have key : ∀ k : Fin d,
827	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree k)
828	          ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
829	        = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
830	            ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
831	    intro k
832	    rw [hQhom]
833	    exact SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom _ k
834	  -- LHS: `map (ιFree i) ≫ map (matrixEnd M)` collapses to `map (ιFree i ≫ matrixEnd M)`,
835	  -- then `ιFree_matrixEnd` turns it into a row sum, distributed by additivity of the pullback.
836	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree i))
837	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEnd M)),
838	    ← Functor.map_comp]
839	  -- `erw` (defeq matching) is needed to see `ιFree i ≫ matrixEnd M` under `(pullback p).map`.
840	  erw [ιFree_matrixEnd M i]
841	  erw [Functor.map_sum]
842	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
843	  -- RHS: cancel `map (ιFree i) ≫ Q.hom` into `pullbackObjUnitToUnit ≫ ιFree i`, then expand.
844	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree i))
845	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom,
846	    key i]
847	  erw [Category.assoc]
848	  erw [ιFree_matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)).mapMatrix M) i]
849	  erw [Preadditive.comp_sum]
850	  -- Match term by term: each entry reduces to the scalar atom `scalarEnd_pullback`.
851	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun k _ => ?_)
852	  erw [Functor.map_comp]
853	  rw [Category.assoc, key k]
854	  erw [reassoc_of% scalarEnd_pullback p (M k i)]
855	  erw [Category.assoc]
856	
857	/-- The action of `matrixEndRect M` on the `j`-th free injection: `ιFree j ≫ matrixEndRect M`
858	expands as the sum over rows `∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ ιFree k`. Project-local helper
859	for `matrixEndRect_pullback` (rectangular analogue of `ιFree_matrixEnd`). -/
860	lemma ιFree_matrixEndRect {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤))
861	    (j : Fin r) :
862	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) j ≫ matrixEndRect M
863	      = ∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) k := by
864	  rw [matrixEndRect, SheafOfModules.ιFree]
865	  simp only [SheafOfModules.free]
866	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv, biproduct.isoCoproduct_hom,
867	    ← Category.assoc, Sigma.ι_desc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.lift_desc]
868	  rfl
869	
870	/-- **Rectangular matrix homomorphism naturality under pullback**
871	(`lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback`). For `p : T ⟶ S` and a `d × r` matrix `M` of global
872	sections, the pullback of `matrixEndRect M` is, after the free-pullback comparisons
873	`Q_r = pullbackFreeIso p (Fin r)` and `Q_d = pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)`, the rectangular
874	homomorphism of the base-changed matrix `p^♯ M`:
875	`(pullback p).map (matrixEndRect M) = Q_r.hom ≫ matrixEndRect (p^♯ M) ≫ Q_d.inv`.
876	Identical skeleton to the square `matrixEnd_pullback`, reducing on each one-element
877	biproduct component to the scalar atom `scalarEnd_pullback`. Project-local. -/
878	lemma matrixEndRect_pullback {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) {d r : ℕ}
879	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
880	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEndRect M)
881	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin r)).hom ≫
882	        matrixEndRect (M.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p))) ≫
883	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv := by
884	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final p
885	  -- Reduce to the naturality square (cancel the trailing `Q_d.inv`).
886	  rw [← Category.assoc, Iso.eq_comp_inv]
887	  -- Check the two maps out of the coproduct `(pullback p).obj (free (Fin r))` agree on each
888	  -- free injection (the cofan of the preserved colimit).
889	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext
890	    (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit (Scheme.Modules.pullback p) _ _
891	      (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin r))) _ _ (fun i => ?_)
892	  simp only [cofan_mk_inj, Cofan.mk_pt]
893	  -- the source/target free-pullback comparisons in their Mathlib form
894	  have hQr : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin r)).hom
895	      = (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) (Fin r)).hom := rfl
896	  have hQd : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
897	      = (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) (Fin d)).hom := rfl
898	  have key_r : ∀ k : Fin r,
899	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree k)
900	          ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin r)).hom
901	        = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
902	            ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
903	    intro k
904	    rw [hQr]
905	    exact SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom _ k
906	  have key_d : ∀ k : Fin d,
907	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree k)
908	          ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
909	        = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
910	            ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
911	    intro k
912	    rw [hQd]
913	    exact SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom _ k
914	  -- LHS: collapse to a row sum via `ιFree_matrixEndRect`, distributed by additivity.
915	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree i))
916	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEndRect M)),
917	    ← Functor.map_comp]
918	  erw [ιFree_matrixEndRect M i]
919	  erw [Functor.map_sum]
920	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
921	  -- RHS: cancel `map (ιFree i) ≫ Q_r.hom` into `pullbackObjUnitToUnit ≫ ιFree i`, expand.
922	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree i))
923	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin r)).hom,
924	    key_r i]
925	  erw [Category.assoc]
926	  erw [ιFree_matrixEndRect (M.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p))) i]
927	  erw [Preadditive.comp_sum]
928	  -- Match term by term: each entry reduces to the scalar atom `scalarEnd_pullback`.
929	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun k _ => ?_)
930	  erw [Functor.map_comp]
931	  rw [Category.assoc, key_d k]
932	  erw [reassoc_of% scalarEnd_pullback p (M k i)]
933	  erw [Category.assoc]
934	
935	/-- The chart quotient is, definitionally, the rectangular matrix homomorphism of the
936	injected universal matrix: `u^I = matrixEndRect ((ΓSpecIso R^I).inv X^I)`. Project-local —
937	the bridge between `chartQuotientMap` and the `matrixEndRect` API. -/
938	lemma chartQuotientMap_eq_matrixEndRect (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
939	    chartQuotientMap d r I hI
940	      = matrixEndRect ((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
941	          ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso
942	            (CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ))).inv)) := rfl
943	
944	/-! ### Entry extraction and the matrix presentation of free-sheaf morphisms
945	
946	(The block below was moved up from the chart-transport section so that the chart-loci
947	covering proof `chartLocus_isOpenCover` — which precedes that section — can use it.) -/

949	/-- The `p`-th **projection of the free sheaf** `O_X^d ⟶ O_X`, through the

2733	\begin{lemma}[The chart locus of a pullback contains the preimage of the chart locus]
2734	  \label{lem:gr_chartLocus_rqPullback}
2735	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartLocus_rqPullback}
2736	  \uses{def:chartLocus, lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback, lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp,
2737	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
2738	  For \(\psi : T' \to T\) and a rank-\(d\) quotient \(y\) on \(T\), the \(\psi\)-preimage of
2739	  the \(I\)-chart locus of \(y\) is contained in the \(I\)-chart locus of the pulled-back
2740	  quotient:
2741	  \[
2742	    \psi^{-1}\bigl(\mathrm{chartLocus}(y, I)\bigr)
2743	      \;\le\; \mathrm{chartLocus}(\psi^{*}y, I).
2744	  \]
2745	\end{lemma}
2746	\begin{proof}
2747	  \uses{lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback, lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp,
2748	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
2749	  Let \(U = \psi^{-1}(\mathrm{chartLocus}(y, I))\). The inclusion \(U \hookrightarrow T'\)
2750	  followed by \(\psi\) factors through the chart-locus inclusion
2751	  \(\iota : \mathrm{chartLocus}(y, I) \hookrightarrow T\), over which the chart composite
2752	  \(s_I \circ y\) pulls back to an isomorphism (\cref{def:chartLocus}). Hence its pullback
2753	  along the factoring composite is an isomorphism
2754	  (\cref{lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp}), and the pullback pseudofunctor comparison
2755	  (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}) together with the chart-composite bridge
2756	  (\cref{lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback}) identifies it with the pullback to \(U\) of the
2757	  chart composite of \(\psi^{*}y\). So \(s_I \circ (\psi^{*}y)\) is invertible over \(U\),
2758	  i.e.\ \(U\) is one of the opens whose supremum defines
2759	  \(\mathrm{chartLocus}(\psi^{*}y, I)\); taking the supremum gives
2760	  \(U \le \mathrm{chartLocus}(\psi^{*}y, I)\).
2761	\end{proof}
