2763	\begin{theorem}
2764	
2765	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) represents the Grassmannian functor]
2766	  \label{thm:grassmannian_universal_property}
2767	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.represents}
2768	  \uses{def:grassmannian_functor, def:tautological_quotient,
2769	    def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_affine_chart,
2770	    def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle}
2771	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
2772	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
2773	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L566).
2774	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2775	  % "Show that $\grass(r,d)$ together with the quotient
2776	  %  $u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$ represents the
2777	  %  contravariant functor
2778	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
2779	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
2780	  %  set of all equivalence classes
2781	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
2782	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$.
2783	  %  Therefore, $\quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$
2784	  %  exists, and equals $\grass(r,d)$."
2785	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2).}
2786	  The Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}),
2787	  together with the tautological quotient \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\)
2788	  (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}), represents the functor
2789	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:grassmannian_functor}). That is, for
2790	  every scheme \(T\) the natural map
2791	  \[
2792	    \operatorname{Hom}(T, \mathrm{Gr}(r,d))
2793	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2794	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T),
2795	    \qquad
2796	    \bigl(\,\varphi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\,\bigr)
2797	      \;\longmapsto\;
2798	    \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U},\, \varphi^{*}u \rangle,
2799	  \]
2800	  is a bijection, natural in \(T\). Consequently the Quot scheme
2801	  \(\quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/
2802	  \mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) of rank-\(d\) quotients of the trivial rank-\(r\) sheaf
2803	  exists and equals \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\); the tautological quotient is the
2804	  universal family.
2805	\end{theorem}
2806	
2807	\begin{proof}
2808	  \uses{def:tautological_quotient, def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf,
2809	    def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix,
2810	    def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle, def:gr_glued_scheme,
2811	    def:grassmannian_functor, lem:tautologicalQuotient_epi,
2812	    def:grPointOfRankQuotient, lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover, def:chartLocus,
2813	    lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map, def:gr_tautologicalRankQuotient,
2814	    def:gr_chartMorphism, def:gr_universalQuotient_restrictionIso,
2815	    lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism, lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback,
2816	    lem:gr_chartLocus_rqPullback, lem:gr_chartMorphism_glue_compat}
2817	  Naturality of \(\varphi \mapsto \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u
2818	  \rangle\) is immediate: pullback is functorial and \(\varphi^{*}u\) is again a
2819	  surjection onto a rank-\(d\) locally free sheaf, so the assignment defines a
2820	  morphism of functors \(\operatorname{Hom}(-,\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)) \to
2821	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\). It remains to show it is a bijection for each
2822	  \(T\); we exhibit an inverse, constructed Zariski-locally on \(T\) and glued.
2823	
2824	  \emph{From a quotient to a morphism.} Let \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle \in
2825	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)\), so \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow
2826	  \mathcal{F}\) with \(\mathcal{F}\) locally free of rank \(d\). For a size-\(d\)
2827	  subset \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) let \(T_I \subseteq T\) be the locus where
2828	  the composite
2829	  \[
2830	    \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}
2831	      \overset{\ \iota_I\ }{\hookrightarrow} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
2832	      \xrightarrow{\ q\ } \mathcal{F},
2833	  \]
2834	  with \(\iota_I\) the inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates, is an
2835	  isomorphism. This locus is open: in a local trivialisation of \(\mathcal{F}\)
2836	  the composite is a \(d \times d\) matrix and \(T_I\) is the non-vanishing locus
2837	  of its determinant. The \(T_I\) cover \(T\): at any point \(t\), the
2838	  \(d\)-dimensional fibre quotient \(q(t) : \kappa(t)^{r} \twoheadrightarrow
2839	  \mathcal{F}\otimes\kappa(t)\) is surjective, so some \(d\) of the \(r\) standard
2840	  basis vectors map to a basis of the \(d\)-dimensional fibre, i.e.\ \(t \in T_I\)
2841	  for that \(I\) (by Nakayama the isomorphism then holds in a neighbourhood).
2842	
2843	  On \(T_I\), trivialise \(\mathcal{F}\) using \(\iota_I^{*}\): the quotient
2844	  becomes a surjection \(q_I : \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,d}\)
2845	  represented by a \(d \times r\) matrix \(M^I\) whose \(I\)-th minor \(M^I_I\) is
2846	  the identity \(1_{d \times d}\) -- exactly the shape of the universal matrix
2847	  \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}). The \(d(r-d)\) free entries of \(M^I\)
2848	  are global functions on \(T_I\) and hence determine a unique
2849	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to
2850	  \Gamma(T_I, \mathcal{O}_{T_I})\) sending \(x^I_{p,q}\) to the corresponding entry
2851	  of \(M^I\), i.e.\ a unique morphism \(\varphi_I : T_I \to U^I\)
2852	  (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) with \(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\) and thus
2853	  \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I = q_I\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}).
2854	
2855	  \emph{Gluing.} On the overlap \(T_I \cap T_J\) the two trivialisations differ by
2856	  the change-of-basis \((M^I_J)^{-1}\), which is exactly the bundle transition
2857	  \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) of \(\mathcal{U}\)
2858	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}); concretely
2859	  \(M^J = (M^I_J)^{-1} M^I = \varphi_I^{*}\theta_{I,J}(X^J)\)
2860	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}). Hence
2861	  \(\varphi_I\) and \(\varphi_J\) land in the overlap charts and agree there after
2862	  the transition \(\theta_{I,J}\) -- this is the overlap compatibility
2863	  \cref{lem:gr_chartMorphism_glue_compat} -- so by the cocycle condition
2864	  (\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}) the \(\varphi_I\) glue to a single morphism
2865	  \(\varphi = \mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle) :
2866	  T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:grPointOfRankQuotient},
2867	  \cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}). This morphism is the candidate inverse; that it
2868	  is a two-sided inverse is the content of the two laws below.
2869	
2870	  \emph{Existence (the law \(\mathrm{right\_inv}\)):
2871	  \(\varphi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle \sim \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\).}
2872	  The equivalence is checked over the chart cover \(\{T_I\}\). Over \(T_I\) the
2873	  morphism \(\varphi\) restricts to \(\varphi_I\) followed by the chart immersion
2874	  \(\iota_I\) (\cref{def:gr_chartMorphism}), and the restriction of the universal
2875	  quotient sheaf along \(\iota_I\) is identified with the free rank-\(d\) sheaf by
2876	  the chart restriction isomorphism
2877	  \(\phi_I : \iota_I^{*}\mathcal{U} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
2878	  (\cref{def:gr_universalQuotient_restrictionIso}). Through this identification
2879	  \(\varphi^{*}u\) becomes \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I\), which by construction of the chart
2880	  morphism (\(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\)) equals \(q|_{T_I}\) in the
2881	  \(I\)-trivialisation (\cref{def:gr_chartMorphism}, \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}).
2882	  The witnessing isomorphisms \(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{F} \cong \mathcal{F}\) so
2883	  produced agree on overlaps -- the class is determined by \(\ker q\), which the
2884	  trivialisations match -- and assemble to a single isomorphism over \(T\), giving
2885	  \(\varphi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle \sim \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\).
2886	
2887	  \emph{Uniqueness (the law \(\mathrm{left\_inv}\)):
2888	  \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle) = \psi\)
2889	  for every \(\psi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\).} It suffices to check the equality
2890	  after restricting to each chart locus
2891	  \(\mathrm{chartLocus}(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle, I)\) of the
2892	  pulled-back tautological quotient. Over such a locus the composite of its
2893	  inclusion with \(\psi\) factors through the \(\psi\)-preimage of the tautological
2894	  chart locus, which is contained in that locus by
2895	  \cref{lem:gr_chartLocus_rqPullback}; the chart-composite bridge
2896	  \cref{lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback} identifies the chart data of
2897	  \(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle\) with the \(\psi\)-pullback of the
2898	  tautological chart data, so the matrix presenting it there is the \(\psi\)-image
2899	  of the universal matrix \(X^I\). Hence the chart morphism classifying
2900	  \(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle\) over this locus coincides with the
2901	  restriction of \(\psi\) into the chart \(U^I\) (\cref{lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism}).
2902	  As the chart loci cover, the glued morphism
2903	  \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle)\) equals
2904	  \(\psi\) by the uniqueness clause of the glue-data universal property.
2905	
2906	  Existence and uniqueness exhibit \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}\) as a two-sided
2907	  inverse of \(\varphi \mapsto \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u\rangle\),
2908	  so the latter is bijective.
2909	
2910	  Therefore \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) with \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\) is a fine
2911	  moduli space: it represents \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\), so
2912	  \(\quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/
2913	  \mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) exists and equals \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\).
2914	\end{proof}

1140	lemma matrixEnd_eq_matrixEndRect {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}

1153	    (mB : Fin e → Fin d →
1154	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)) :
1155	    biproduct.matrix (f := fun _ : Fin n => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)
1156	        (g := fun _ : Fin e => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) mA ≫ biproduct.matrix mB

1224	/-! ### Epimorphisms between free sheaves split over affines
1225	
1226	The Nakayama/covering step of `chartLocus_isOpenCover` needs pointwise (fibre-level)
1227	surjectivity of an epimorphism of sheaves of modules. Over an affine base this needs no
1228	stalk theory at all: the tilde functor `ModuleCat R ⥤ (Spec R).Modules` is fully
1229	faithful and additive, identifies the free sheaf with the tilde of the free module
1230	(`tildeFinsupp`), epimorphisms of modules are surjections, and free modules are
1231	projective — so any epimorphism between free sheaves on `Spec R` is *split*, and its
1232	presenting matrix admits a right inverse over the global sections. -/
1233	
1234	/-- **Epimorphisms between free sheaves of modules on `Spec R` split**: the splitting is
1235	transported through the fully faithful `tilde.functor` from the projectivity of the free
1236	module `Fin d →₀ R`. Project-local. -/
1237	lemma exists_section_of_epi_free_spec {R : CommRingCat.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
1238	    (ψ : SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
1239	      SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) [Epi ψ] :
1240	    ∃ Φ : SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶
1241	        SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r),
1242	      Φ ≫ ψ = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.free (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) := by
1243	  -- the tilde-conjugate of `ψ` and its module-level preimage under full faithfulness
1244	  let ψt : tilde (ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin r →₀ ↥R)) ⟶ tilde (ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin d →₀ ↥R)) :=
1245	    (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom ≫ ψ ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv
1246	  let g : ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin r →₀ ↥R) ⟶ ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin d →₀ ↥R) :=
1247	    tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor.preimage ψt
1248	  have hg : tilde.map g = ψt := tilde.fullyFaithfulFunctor.map_preimage ψt
1249	  haveI hiso_r : IsIso (tildeFinsupp (R := R) (Fin r)).hom := inferInstance
1250	  haveI hiso_d : IsIso (tildeFinsupp (R := R) (Fin d)).inv := inferInstance
1251	  haveI hψt : Epi ψt := by
1252	    -- fully explicit: the `(Spec R).Modules` / `(Spec (.of ↥R)).Modules` defeq blocks
1253	    -- automatic instance search across the composite
1254	    haveI h1 : Epi (ψ ≫ (tildeFinsupp (Fin d)).inv) :=
1255	      @epi_comp _ _ _ _ _ ψ ‹Epi ψ› _ (IsIso.epi_of_iso _)
1256	    exact @epi_comp _ _ _ _ _ (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom (IsIso.epi_of_iso _) _ h1
1257	  haveI hge : Epi g := by
1258	    refine (tilde.functor R).epi_of_epi_map ?_
1259	    show Epi (tilde.map g)
1260	    rw [hg]
1261	    exact hψt

1263	  -- a module-level section of `g` by projectivity of the free module

1266	    (tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom, ?_⟩
1267	  have hcomp : ModuleCat.ofHom w ≫ g = 𝟙 (ModuleCat.of ↥R (Fin d →₀ ↥R)) :=

1277	        ((tildeFinsupp (Fin r)).hom ≫ ψ) := by simp only [Category.assoc]
